Title: Junior Data Scientist / AI Engineer
HTML:
Dale Consulting ricerca Junior Data Scientist / AI Engineer per un importante cliente internazionale, da inserire in un team strutturato e tecnologicamente avanzato.


| CHI SIAMO

Dale Consulting, società di Consulenza Informatica, offre servizi ai principali player internazionali in ambito tecnologico, supportando le aziende nella realizzazione e gestione di soluzioni innovative e ad alto valore strategico.

Società dedita all’eccellenza e all'impegno costante per supportare le sfide dei propri clienti, fornendo loro soluzioni customizzate e contribuendo alla realizzazione e gestione di prodotti, servizi e strumenti di alto valore strategico.


| DI COSA TI OCCUPERAI

Entrerai in un contesto enterprise ad alta complessità, dove avrai l’opportunità di crescere e sviluppare competenze in ambito Data Science e AI, lavorando a stretto contatto con professionisti senior.


Sarai coinvolto in attività di analisi dati, sviluppo di modelli e supporto alla realizzazione di soluzioni data-driven.


In particolare ti occuperai di:


  • Supportare lo sviluppo di modelli di machine learning;
  • Analizzare ed elaborare dataset;
  • Scrivere codice in Python per attività di analisi e modellazione;
  • Collaborare con il team nello sviluppo e nell’integrazione delle soluzioni;
  • Partecipare alle attività di validazione e testing dei modelli;
  • Contribuire alla documentazione tecnica.


| COSA RICHIEDIAMO

  • Laurea in discipline STEM (Informatica, Ingegneria, Matematica, Fisica o affini);
  • Conoscenza base di Python;
  • Familiarità con concetti di analisi dati e machine learning;
  • Conoscenza base di SQL;
  • Interesse per il mondo dei dati e dell’intelligenza artificiale.


| NICE TO HAVE

  • Progetti accademici o personali in ambito Data Science;
  • Conoscenza di librerie Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn);
  • Familiarità con strumenti di data visualization;
  • Esperienze di stage o tirocinio in ambito dati.


| COSA OFFRIAMO?

  • Percorso di crescita strutturato con affiancamento a figure senior;
  • Coinvolgimento in progetti complessi e innovativi;
  • Ambiente dinamico e orientato all’apprendimento.


Modalità di lavoro: ibrida (3+2) – Milano

Contratto a tempo indeterminato e RAL commisurata alla reale esperienza


L’offerta si rivolge a candidati ambosessi, nel rispetto del Codice delle pari opportunità D.Lgs. 11 aprile 2006, N. 198 e ss.mm.ii.

Title: AI Engineer Junior
Description:
Stiamo supportando un nostro cliente nella ricerca di un/una AI Engineer Junior da inserire in un progetto innovativo legato all’applicazione dell’intelligenza artificiale al contesto manifatturiero.

La risorsa entrerà a far parte di un’iniziativa ad alto impatto, con l’obiettivo di sviluppare soluzioni AI a supporto dei processi produttivi, lavorando su dati industriali reali.




Cosa farà

Il/la candidato/a contribuirà allo sviluppo di sistemi per:

analisi delle performance produttive e individuazione di pattern e anomalie
supporto alla costruzione di modelli predittivi (es. efficienza, fermi macchina)
analisi dei dati di pianificazione e sviluppo di semplici simulazioni
implementazione di logiche di supporto alle decisioni operative

L’attività si baserà su dati provenienti da produzione, manutenzione, qualità, pianificazione ed ERP.




Requisiti

esperienza di 1–2 anni, anche tramite stage o apprendistato, in ambito AI, Data Science o Machine Learning
buona conoscenza di Python (es. pandas, basi di machine learning)
familiarità con dati strutturati e non strutturati
interesse per integrazione con sistemi (API, database)
approccio pratico e orientamento all’apprendimento

Plus

conoscenza di LLM o strumenti di intelligenza artificiale applicata
esperienza su progetti dati (anche accademici)
interesse per il settore industriale/manifatturiero




Informazioni:

possibilità di evoluzione verso un ruolo AI Engineer più senior
RAL indicativa: 28.000 – 34.000 €, commisurata all’esperienza
Sede provincia di Cremona

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

To help fuel that growth, we need a Data Scientist to join our Italian Pricing & Underwriting team, that is the strategic engine behind Prima's growth.

As part of this team, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping our strategy and strengthening our competitive edge. Using data, analytical thinking, machine learning and business acumen, you’ll help predict the future and set the right prices, keeping our offering both attractive and sustainable. You’ll work with your data science tools and skills to bring real-world impact, collaborating with cross-functional teams to turn insights into action and fuel innovation across the company.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details

What You’ll Do


Leverage Machine Learning and advanced data analytics to identify risks and set the right price for each client;
Analyze and interpret complex data to support strategic decision-making;
Contribute directly to the company’s growth and long-term success through actionable insights.


What We’re Looking For


Strong academic background in Mathematics, Physics, Mathematical Engineering, Physical Engineering, Data Science, Informatics, Computer Engineering or Statistics;
All experience levels, including recent graduates with no prior work experience;
Excellent quantitative, logical, and analytical skills;
Curiosity, proactivity, and innovative thinking to drive new ideas and solutions.


Nice-to-have


Possess a solid foundation in code development and machine learning;
Know how to create, manipulate and analyze large datasets.


Curious to know more? Apply or check out this video.

Why you’ll love it here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks.

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy full flexibility – work from home, the office or a mix of both. Plus, work from anywhere for up to 30 days a year.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.

Title: Junior Research & Data/AI Engineer
Description:
About ION And Our Research Mission

At ION, research is not separate from product — it is the engine of innovation.

Our mission is to transform the world of financial and economic intelligence through rigorous, data-driven research embedded directly in product development.

ION’s Core Banking and Analytics platforms provide access to one of the most comprehensive data ecosystems in the financial sector — spanning transactional, accounting, behavioral, textual, and unstructured data from across multiple banking institutions.

Our team drives the scientific and technological development of ION’s core financial products — including credit risk scoring, early warning systems, portfolio monitoring, and digital finance analytics — while advancing the global state of knowledge through collaborations and participation in top-tier conferences.

About The Role

We are seeking a Junior Research & Data/AI Engineer to join our multidisciplinary research and engineering team.

This hybrid role combines quantitative research and data engineering skills to design, implement, and deploy innovative models and data-driven solutions that power ION’s financial products.

You will work in an environment where academic rigor meets applied innovation, contributing to model development, data pipelines, and machine learning workflows.

This position offers opportunities to collaborate with ION Labs hosted at Bocconi University, HEC Paris, and leading U.S. institutions, and to learn from senior experts through joint projects and internal training.

Key Responsibilities


Research & Modeling
Support applied research initiatives in economics, finance, and data science.
Implement and validate models using econometrics, machine learning, and forecasting techniques.
Explore innovative methodologies and integrate diverse data sources (transactional, textual, behavioral).
Data & AI Engineering
Design and maintain scalable data pipelines using PySpark and Databricks.
Implement and optimize ML workflows for production environments.
Collaborate with researchers and product teams to ensure reproducibility and scalability.
Collaboration & Learning
Participate in internal seminars and training sessions on econometrics, data modeling, and AI.
Contribute to documentation and explainability for internal stakeholders and regulatory compliance.


Required Qualifications & Skills


Master’s degree in Economics, Finance, Data Science, Computer Science, AI, or related quantitative field.
Solid foundation in econometrics, machine learning, and data analysis.
Proficiency in Python and familiarity with big data frameworks (PySpark, Databricks).Understanding of ML pipelines, cloud-based architectures, and data governance.
Strong analytical mindset, problem-solving skills, and ability to work in a multidisciplinary team.
Good communication skills and willingness to learn advanced modeling and engineering techniques.


Why Join ION

Work at the intersection of finance, data science, and AI.

Access one of the richest financial datasets in Europe.

Collaborate with top universities and global research networks.

Contribute to real-world solutions with impact on financial products.

Be part of a team that values rigor, innovation, and intellectual curiosity.

Important notes

According to the Italian Law (L.68/99) please note that candidates from the disability list will be given priority.

Due to the high volume of applications, only those candidates that meet the required criteria for selection will be contacted.

Please do not apply if you don’t currently have the legal right to work in Italy, or if that right is just about to expire, or if that right has restrictions imposed which would prevent you from undertaking a full-time employment contract.

Title: EXPERT POWER QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS AND PREDICTIVE ANALYTICS (IT)
Description:
Job Description

Come Expert Power Quantitative Analysis and Predictive Analytics farai partedel business Power Generation & Marketing della Direzione Generale Global Natural Resources.

L’area si occupa della gestione e dell’ottimizzazione del parco di generazione elettrica italiano di Eni, del presidio del rischio commodity, dello sviluppo di attività commerciali e di trading, nonché dell’analisi di nuove opportunità di business e dell’evoluzione del mercato elettrico.

In questo contesto, lavorerai su modelli quantitativi e strumenti analitici integrati nei processi decisionali del business, contribuendo allo sviluppo di soluzioni previsionali, modelli di ottimizzazione, metodologie di pricing e analisi di mercato applicate al settore elettrico, all’interno di un team giovane, dinamico e stimolante.

Attività Principali


Sviluppare, implementare e manutenere modelli quantitativi relativi alle attività di Risk Management, Asset Management, Portfolio Management e Trading
Contribuire allo sviluppo di modelli di ottimizzazione per la gestione a mercato del parco produttivo elettrico di Eni
Sviluppare modelli di previsione dei prezzi dell’energia elettrica e delle principali variabili di mercato
Realizzare analisi quantitative, statistiche e data-driven per interpretare le dinamiche del mercato elettrico italiano e l’evoluzione dei fondamentali
Sviluppare modelli previsionali per i consumi elettrici di grandi clienti industriali
Elaborare metodologie e strumenti di pricing B2B, integrando le analisi profili di consumo, rischio, marginalità e scenari di mercato
Contribuire alla costruzione di strumenti analitici, dashboard, librerie e workflow in Python, con l’obiettivo di rendere i processi quantitativi dell’unità più scalabili, robusti e replicabili
Produrre analisi quantitative per lo sviluppo di nuovi business, iniziative strategiche e progetti speciali.


Caratteristiche Richieste


Laurea magistrale in Matematica, Fisica, Statistica, Ingegneria matematica; potranno essere presi in considerazione titoli di studio differenti, a valle di significativa esperienza nell’ambito
Esperienza di almeno 3 anni maturata in ruoli quantitativi, data science, market analysis, trading analytics, risk analytics o portfolio analytics nel settore elettrico, energy trading, commodity o utilities
Esperienza nell’analisi quantitativa del mercato elettrico italiano e/o europeo, con conoscenza operativa delle principali dinamiche di formazione dei prezzi, dei mercati spot e forward, dei driver fondamentali della domanda e dell’offerta, della generazione rinnovabile e termoelettrica, dei vincoli di sistema e delle logiche di dispacciamento
Ottima capacità di programmazione in Python, con esperienza nello sviluppo di codice strutturato, mantenibile e orientato all’analisi quantitativa
Solida conoscenza di metodi matematici, statistici e probabilistici applicati a serie storiche, forecasting, ottimizzazione e analisi dati
Familiarità con librerie Python per data analysis, modellistica e machine learning, quali pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, statsmodels, PyTorch/TensorFlow o equivalenti
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese
Disponibilità alla mobilità


Completano Il Profilo


PhD in matematica, fisica o discipline quantitative affini
Esperienza diretta nel mercato elettrico italiano o europeo
Conoscenza di modelli di ottimizzazione, dispatching, power forecasting o pricing
Conoscenza di metodologie di finanza quantitativa, modelli stocastici, risk modelling o valuation
Esperienza nello sviluppo di modelli di machine learning applicati a forecasting, anomaly detection, clustering o scenari di mercato
Familiarità con database SQL, cloud environment, strumenti di versioning e pratiche di sviluppo collaborativo.
Contratto di lavoro a Tempo Indeterminato
Il nostro contratto collettivo nazionale di lavoro di riferimento è il CCNL Energia e Petrolio


Sede

San Donato Milanese (MI)

Promuoviamo forme flessibili di lavoro, offrendo opzioni di Smart working ed orari flessibili al fine di sostenere il maggior equilibrio tra vita professionale e personale.

Proponiamo


Proponiamo una retribuzione annua lorda compresa tra 47.000 € e 50.000 €
L’offerta finale sarà formulata in coerenza con l’esperienza professionale, le competenze tecniche e trasversali possedute dalla risorsa funzionali per il ruolo e apprezzabili per l’azienda
L’Azienda in presenza di esperienze professionali e competenze tecniche e trasversali particolarmente rilevanti potrà valutare un’offerta con un livello di inquadramento/RAL migliorativo
Sistema di reward e programma articolato di benefits e iniziative di welfare in ambito sanitario, previdenziale, di supporto al reddito e per la conciliazione vita-lavoro. Scopri di più: link welfare
Ambiente di lavoro in cui ogni risorsa possa sentirsi riconosciuta e apprezzata nella propria unicità


Le candidature ritenute di maggior interesse saranno contattate per le fasi successive.

Per informazioni sul processo di selezione e consigli utili puoi cliccare qui .

Chi Siamo

Eni è una società integrata lungo l’intera catena del valore e impegnata con azioni concrete a fornire energia sempre più sicura, diversificata e a emissioni progressivamente ridotte.

Il modello di business punta alla creazione di valore di lungo termine per tutti gli stakeholder.

Le nostre persone sono il motore di questa strategia, il cui talento, know-how e impegno guidano una crescita costante e trasformano la nostra visione in progressi concreti.

Global Natural Resources è la Direzione che si occupa di gestire e sviluppare le leve tecniche, operative e di ingegneria per la realizzazione dei grandi progetti della Società. È integrata con il business Power Generation & Marketing e le attività del Trading Oil.

  • Contribuire allo sviluppo di modelli di ottimizzazione per la gestione a mercato del parco produttivo elettrico di Eni
  • Sviluppare modelli di previsione dei prezzi dell’energia elettrica e delle principali variabili di mercato
  • Realizzare analisi quantitative, statistiche e data-driven per interpretare le dinamiche del mercato elettrico italiano e l’evoluzione dei fondamentali
  • Sviluppare modelli previsionali per i consumi elettrici di grandi clienti industriali
  • Elaborare metodologie e strumenti di pricing B2B, integrando le analisi profili di consumo, rischio, marginalità e scenari di mercato
  • Contribuire alla costruzione di strumenti analitici, dashboard, librerie e workflow in Python, con l’obiettivo di rendere i processi quantitativi dell’unità più scalabili, robusti e replicabili
  • Produrre analisi quantitative per lo sviluppo di nuovi business, iniziative strategiche e progetti speciali.

Caratteristiche Richieste

  • Laurea magistrale in Matematica, Fisica, Statistica, Ingegneria matematica; potranno essere presi in considerazione titoli di studio differenti, a valle di significativa esperienza nell’ambito
  • Esperienza di almeno 3 anni maturata in ruoli quantitativi, data science, market analysis, trading analytics, risk analytics o portfolio analytics nel settore elettrico, energy trading, commodity o utilities
  • Esperienza nell’analisi quantitativa del mercato elettrico italiano e/o europeo, con conoscenza operativa delle principali dinamiche di formazione dei prezzi, dei mercati spot e forward, dei driver fondamentali della domanda e dell’offerta, della generazione rinnovabile e termoelettrica, dei vincoli di sistema e delle logiche di dispacciamento
  • Ottima capacità di programmazione in Python, con esperienza nello sviluppo di codice strutturato, mantenibile e orientato all’analisi quantitativa
  • Solida conoscenza di metodi matematici, statistici e probabilistici applicati a serie storiche, forecasting, ottimizzazione e analisi dati
  • Familiarità con librerie Python per data analysis, modellistica e machine learning, quali pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, statsmodels, PyTorch/TensorFlow o equivalenti
  • Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese
  • Disponibilità alla mobilità

Completano Il Profilo

  • PhD in matematica, fisica o discipline quantitative affini
  • Esperienza diretta nel mercato elettrico italiano o europeo
  • Conoscenza di modelli di ottimizzazione, dispatching, power forecasting o pricing
  • Conoscenza di metodologie di finanza quantitativa, modelli stocastici, risk modelling o valuation
  • Esperienza nello sviluppo di modelli di machine learning applicati a forecasting, anomaly detection, clustering o scenari di mercato
  • Familiarità con database SQL, cloud environment, strumenti di versioning e pratiche di sviluppo collaborativo.
  • Contratto di lavoro a Tempo Indeterminato
  • Il nostro contratto collettivo nazionale di lavoro di riferimento è il CCNL Energia e Petrolio

Sede

San Donato Milanese (MI)

Promuoviamo forme flessibili di lavoro, offrendo opzioni di Smart working ed orari flessibili al fine di sostenere il maggior equilibrio tra vita professionale e personale.

Proponiamo

  • Proponiamo una retribuzione annua lorda compresa tra 47.000 € e 50.000 €
  • L’offerta finale sarà formulata in coerenza con l’esperienza professionale, le competenze tecniche e trasversali possedute dalla risorsa funzionali per il ruolo e apprezzabili per l’azienda
  • L’Azienda in presenza di esperienze professionali e competenze tecniche e trasversali particolarmente rilevanti potrà valutare un’offerta con un livello di inquadramento/RAL migliorativo
  • Sistema di reward e programma articolato di benefits e iniziative di welfare in ambito sanitario, previdenziale, di supporto al reddito e per la conciliazione vita-lavoro. Scopri di più: link welfare
  • Ambiente di lavoro in cui ogni risorsa possa sentirsi riconosciuta e apprezzata nella propria unicità

Le candidature ritenute di maggior interesse saranno contattate per le fasi successive.

Per informazioni sul processo di selezione e consigli utili puoi cliccare qui .

Chi Siamo

Eni è una società integrata lungo l’intera catena del valore e impegnata con azioni concrete a fornire energia sempre più sicura, diversificata e a emissioni progressivamente ridotte.

Il modello di business punta alla creazione di valore di lungo termine per tutti gli stakeholder.

Le nostre persone sono il motore di questa strategia, il cui talento, know-how e impegno guidano una crescita costante e trasformano la nostra visione in progressi concreti.

Global Natural Resources è la Direzione che si occupa di gestire e sviluppare le leve tecniche, operative e di ingegneria per la realizzazione dei grandi progetti della Società. È integrata con il business Power Generation & Marketing e le attività del Trading Oil.

Title: Data Scientist GenAI
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

We’re looking for a Data Scientist to join our AI team focused on bringing ML & Generative AI into the heart of our operations (e.g. claims, underwriting, customer support).

In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to redefine the core operative processes, leveraging ML & GenAI techniques to enhance not only efficiency but, most importantly, the overall effectiveness and quality of outcomes.

You’ll collaborate with data scientists, engineers, and business leaders to design and deliver AI-driven products that create tangible business value and redefine the future of ops at Prima.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details

What You’ll Do


Leverage Machine Learning and GenAI to solve complex business problems, making our processes better and faster;
Analyze and interpret complex data to support decision-making;
Contribute directly to Prima’s long-term success and financial impact through concrete solutions.


What We’re Looking For


Strong academic background in quantitative fields such as Math, Physics, Data Science, or Engineering;
All experience levels, including recent graduates with no prior working experience;
Strong analytical and quantitative skills;
Structured problem solving skills and being able to analyze and break down complex problems;
Curiosity, proactivity, quick learning and an innovative mindset.


Nice-to-Have


Hands-on experience with Python and coding best practices;
Experience with machine learning and Generative AI (openAI/Gemini APIs, agentic frameworks, …)
Business proficiency in Italian or Spanish, in addition to English.


Why you’ll love it here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks..

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy full flexibility – work from home, the office or a mix of both. Plus, work from anywhere for up to 30 days a year.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About the Role

Next Ventures is currently supporting one of our clients in Brussels, and we're looking for a few AI Engineers for the role.




This is a freelance contract
2 Years in a production environment is required




Responsibilities

Translate customer challenges into fit-for-purpose AI architectures and pilot prototypes.
Work closely with customer to understand needs and communicate results clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Champion best practices across version control, CI/CD pipelines, and modern software development throughout every engagement.




Requirements

3-5 years of AI/ML engineering experience including B2B customer deliveries.
Strong Python expertise and hands-on proficiency with LLMs and associated libraries (LangChain, LlamaIndex, etc.).
Proven experience deploying scalable AI applications on Azure Cloud, including Azure Data & AI services.
Familiarity with agentic AI frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and MLOps tooling (MLflow, Kubeflow) is a strong plus.
Dutch is a bonus

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
COSMOTE Global Solutions, part of the OTE Group of Companies, is seeking a skilled Data Scientist to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will leverage data analysis, machine learning, and statistical techniques to extract valuable insights that drive informed business decisions and innovation.

Key Responsibilities:


Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights
Develop, test, and implement machine learning models and algorithms
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business challenges and provide data-driven solutions
Create data visualizations and reports to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders
Ensure data quality and integrity throughout the analysis process
Continuously research and apply new data science techniques and technologies


Requirements


Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
Minimum 13 years of previous relavant experience in IT
Strong proficiency in programming languages such as Python or R
Experience with machine learning frameworks and libraries (e.g., TensorFlow, scikit-learn)
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as Azure or AWS is a plus
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal

Title: Data Science Internship
HTML:
Job Location

Brussels

Job Description

Do you have passion for technology and desire to learn and grow continuously? Do you want to belong to one of the most advanced Data Science & Analytics group in the industry, developing sophisticated mathematical models and algorithms and applying Operations Research techniques?

Then join P&G as a Data Science intern for Supply Chain! Through your internship, you will build your Data Science and Analytics skills, develop your ability to influence and think strategically and learn how to work across domains and functions to make P&G’s supply chain more performant, more resilient and more sustainable.

Find out more about IT at P&G at www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ial3aeA9SE.

AS A DATA SCIENCE FOR SUPPLY CHAIN INTERN, YOU WILL:

  • Influence decisions from daily through to strategic ones, made across functions, at all levels of leadership, to have a meaningful impact on the performance, resiliency and environmental footprint of one of the largest CPG companies in the world;
  • Build the next generation of supply chain analytics, using:
    • Mathematical modeling techniques: decision analysis, optimization, machine learning, simulation etc.;
    • Programming languages (at least one of these): Python, SQL, R, Hive/Impala, MATLAB, etc.;
    • Data Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, etc.
    • Work with technology partners to translate your innovations into robust, scaled, analytical solutions;
    • Communicate insights from complex data or algorithms into simple conclusions that will empower others to act based on the insights you derive;
    • Belong to an award-winning Analytics / Operations Research community that is global in scope and operates across P&G’s supply chains.

    Job Qualifications

    YOU ARE THE RIGHT FIT IF YOU:

    • Are currently pursuing a Master's degree in a quantitative field (Engineering, Statistics, Operation Research, Decisions Sciences, Computer Science, Applied Math, Economics, Data Science, etc.);
    • Have strong analytical skills and proficiency in at least one programming language (R, Python, SQL, etc.) or family of techniques (optimization, simulation, machine learning, predictive modeling, etc.);
    • Are adaptable and able to handle multiple priorities effectively, both independently and as part of a collaborative team;
    • Are proficient in English, both written and spoken;

    We are offering internships of at least 3 months throughout the year, as of May 2026.

    If studying at a Belgian university, please make sure you consult with the internship department at your school to agree on the paid internship we offer.

    Your Advantages

    • A temporary contract for the duration of your internship, with an attractive compensation package;
    • Monthly allowance: 2366 EUR brut/month, prorated 13th month based on the months worked during your internship period;
    • A flexible benefits plan to customize to your personal needs and kilometer allowance/public transport reduction;
    • Access to Benefits at work platform: A large benefits platform with employee discounts on renowned shops and online commercial platforms as well as on leisure activities and well-known amusement parks;
    • Working in an international and inclusive environment, with diverse and supporting colleagues;
    • Continuous self-development via coaching, trainings and a general Growth Mindset working environment;
    • A vibrant and modern working environment, supporting employees’ wellbeing:
    • Subsidized company restaurant;
    • On-site vibrant Café for breaks and less formal meetings;
    • On-site fitness room and possibility to join regular group sport activities;
    • Flexibility in terms of working hours;
    • Employee Assistance Program: free professional counseling services, work-life resources, and mental health support.
    • Supporting employee's commute: plenty of parking facilities for both cars and bikes, shower facilities, shuttle between Heizel metro and the office, carpooling community.

    ARE YOU EXCITED ABOUT THE ROLE? DEEP DIVE INTO P&G AND CHECK THE BELOW INSIGHTFUL LINKS:

    • Our hiring process consists of three main steps: 1) application, 2) online assessments and 3) interviews. Click here to get all the tips to be fully prepared.
    • P&G is driven to make life better, not just within the company, but across the globe. Click here to check out how we make a meaningful impact on the world.
    • We are the world’s largest consumer goods company. Click here to discover about our iconic brands.

    At P&G #weseeequal

    We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. At P&G we strive to build a culture where everyone feels welcome, included, and able to bring their full selves to work.

    We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process. Please click here if you require an accommodation during the application process. Please make sure to wait to hear back from us regarding your accommodation before proceeding with the online assessment, we thank you in advance for your patience.

    Job Schedule

    Full time

    Job Number

    R000145698

    Job Segmentation

    Internships

    Starting Pay / Salary Range

    €2.366,00 / month

Title: Freelance Senior Data Scientist Python & Machine Learning (Freelance kan)
Description:
Taken en verantwoordelijkheden

Het data mining-team zoekt een senior profiel voor een dubbele opdracht: enerzijds strategische data-initiatieven versnellen met inhoudelijke expertise, technische richting en coaching, en anderzijds de continue instroom van ad-hoc datavragen structureren, prioriteren en vertalen naar herbruikbare oplossingen en dataproducten.

In deze rol breng je senioriteit, structuur en technische diepgang in het team en ondersteun je de dagelijkse werking en opvolging in afstemming met de verantwoordelijke en andere stakeholders.

Kernverantwoordelijkheden


Strategische projecten & technische leadership
Technische leiding opnemen in complexe datatrajecten, zoals advanced analytics, graph- of network analytics, integraties en architecturale keuzes.
Meebouwen aan aanpak, solutioning en prioriteiten van grotere initiatieven, met aandacht voor haalbaarheid, impact en schaalbaarheid.
Kwaliteitsstandaarden bewaken en uitdragen, waaronder reproduceerbaarheid, documentatie, methodiek en waar relevant engineeringkwaliteit.
Team uplift & co-creatie
Data scientists en analisten coachen en begeleiden via co-creatie, inhoudelijke reviews en het delen van best practices.
Structureel bijdragen aan de verhoging van teamcompetenties op vlak van methodiek, aanpak, kwaliteit en communicatie.
Actief meewerken aan teamafspraken rond definition of done, werkvormen en kennisdeling.
Ad-hoc vraagstroom structureren en productiseren
Overzicht creëren in binnenkomende vragen via intake, slicing, prioritering en heldere statuscommunicatie.
Ad-hoc werk clusteren en waar mogelijk omzetten naar structurele, herbruikbare oplossingen, zoals datasets, analysemethodes, templates en dataproducten.
FAIR-principes toepassen vanuit een data product-perspectief, met focus op kwaliteit en herbruikbaarheid.
Projectmanagement & opvolging
Basis delivery- en projectopvolging opnemen rond scope, milestones, afhankelijkheden en risico's.
Ondersteunen in coördinatie en opvolging om meer stabiliteit te brengen in planning en uitvoering.
Bijdragen aan stakeholder alignment, verwachtingsmanagement, besluitvorming en indien nodig escalaties.



Samenwerking & stakeholders


Nauw samenwerken met data scientists, analisten en waar relevant data engineers en platformstakeholders.
Afstemmen met het Data Platform-team en inhoudelijke partners.
Functioneren in een omgeving met meerdere prioriteiten, waar structuur in intake, opvolging en communicatie essentieel is.



Jouw profiel


Master in IT.
Sterke hands-on ervaring als Data Scientist of ML Engineer, met een duidelijke focus op Python.
Ervaring met data-analyse en datamodellering, onder meer met pandas en scikit-learn, en met het bouwen en verbeteren van ML-modellen in een productiecontext.
Degelijke software engineering-basis: Git, code reviews, CI/CD pipelines, Docker en het opzetten van API's en herbruikbare componenten, bijvoorbeeld met FastAPI.
Kennis van SQL.
Ervaring met cloudomgevingen of infrastructure as code is een plus, bijvoorbeeld Terraform, AWS of GCP.
Je bent sterk in het structureren van onduidelijke vragen en vertaalt die naar een concrete aanpak en opleveringen.
Ervaring met coaching, mentoring en samenwerken in co-creatie, bijvoorbeeld via technische opleidingen, reviews of werken in een Agile/Scrum-context.
Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden: stakeholders meenemen, helder rapporteren en verwachtingen beheren.
Nederlands, Frans en Engels zijn sterk gewenst, bij voorkeur op een hoog niveau.
Kennis van Java is een bijkomende troef.



Pluspunten


Ervaring met data product thinking, governance en kwaliteitsprincipes zoals FAIR, definities, documentatie en herbruikbaarheid.
Ervaring met graph analytics, network analytics of andere domeinen binnen advanced analytics.
Kennis van Databricks.
Ervaring binnen een instelling van sociale zekerheid is een sterke plus.
Eerdere ervaring met secondary data use en fraudedetectie.



Verwachte impact


Een duidelijker intake- en prioriteringsproces voor ad-hoc vragen richting het data mining-team.
Meer herbruikbare en schaalbare output in plaats van losse one-off oplossingen.
Meetbare kwaliteitsverbetering binnen het team via coaching, reviews en methodische afspraken.
Betere voorspelbaarheid en voortgang in belangrijke datatrajecten en strategische initiatieven.



Selectieoefening

Samen met je cv vragen we om ook het antwoord op volgende oefening mee te sturen: leg uit hoe een random forest werkt en in welke situaties je de voorkeur zou geven aan XGBoost of AdaBoost in vergelijking met een Random Forest.

Kandidaturen zonder antwoord, of met een onvoldoende onderbouwd antwoord, worden niet weerhouden.

Aanbod

Je zal deel uitmaken van een groeiende KMO met ruimte voor initiatief en persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Wij zorgen ervoor dat je in een uitdagende maar aangename werkomgeving terecht komt met leuke collega's. Samen met jou stippelen we een carriereplan uit, met aandacht en budget voor bijkomende opleidingingen / certificaties. Je kan rekenen op een motiverend salaris, aangevuld met extralegale voordelen, inclusief bedrijfswagen (of alternatieve vergoeding).

(Freelance is ook mogelijk)

  • Kennis van Databricks.
  • Ervaring binnen een instelling van sociale zekerheid is een sterke plus.
  • Eerdere ervaring met secondary data use en fraudedetectie.


Verwachte impact

  • Een duidelijker intake- en prioriteringsproces voor ad-hoc vragen richting het data mining-team.
  • Meer herbruikbare en schaalbare output in plaats van losse one-off oplossingen.
  • Meetbare kwaliteitsverbetering binnen het team via coaching, reviews en methodische afspraken.
  • Betere voorspelbaarheid en voortgang in belangrijke datatrajecten en strategische initiatieven.


Selectieoefening

Samen met je cv vragen we om ook het antwoord op volgende oefening mee te sturen: leg uit hoe een random forest werkt en in welke situaties je de voorkeur zou geven aan XGBoost of AdaBoost in vergelijking met een Random Forest.

Kandidaturen zonder antwoord, of met een onvoldoende onderbouwd antwoord, worden niet weerhouden.

Aanbod

Je zal deel uitmaken van een groeiende KMO met ruimte voor initiatief en persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Wij zorgen ervoor dat je in een uitdagende maar aangename werkomgeving terecht komt met leuke collega's. Samen met jou stippelen we een carriereplan uit, met aandacht en budget voor bijkomende opleidingingen / certificaties. Je kan rekenen op een motiverend salaris, aangevuld met extralegale voordelen, inclusief bedrijfswagen (of alternatieve vergoeding).

(Freelance is ook mogelijk)

Title: AI Data Scientist
Description:
About Exsolvæ

Exsolvæ is a Brussels-based Data & AI consultancy.

Business is structured around three main areas: Data Foundations, Data Science & AI, and BI & Analytics.




We build AI systems that run in production for Belgian enterprises across finance, pharma, insurance, retail, and energy.




We're looking for a Data Scientist specialised in Artificial Intelligence to design and ship these systems with us.




What we look for?

Technical depth: you've put AI into production and can defend your design choices.
Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Business sense: you understand what decision a model serves before building it.




The Role:

You will design, build, and run AI systems for our clients across Belgium.

Hands-on building, in production, at scale.




What will you do?

Build end-to-end RAG solutions over enterprise data.
Integrate Model Context Protocol (MCP) to connect LLMs with internal tools and data.
Design multi-agent architectures to automate complex workflows.
Fine-tune and evaluate LLMs; implement guardrails for hallucination, toxicity, and relevance.
Deploy to production: API, monitoring, scaling.




The Technical Stack

LLMs: OpenAI GPT-4/4o, Azure OpenAI, Claude, LLaMA, Mistral, HuggingFace.
RAG: LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector stores (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma), Azure AI Search.
MCP / agents: Anthropic MCP, LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, AutoGen, CrewAI.
Classic ML: Scikit-learn, XGBoost, PyTorch, TensorFlow, MLflow.
Infra / deploy: Azure ML, Docker, Kubernetes, FastAPI, Streamlit.
Languages: Python (primary), SQL, TypeScript.




The Mindset we are looking for:

You ship production systems, not POCs.
You adapt your communication to your audience.
You stay rigorous: you don't overclaim.
You take ownership end to end.
You design for trust: evaluation and guardrails from the start.
You mentor through how you work.




Profile & Experience

3–8 years in Data Science, including 1–3 in Artificial Intelligence/LLM.

Mid to senior level.

Right to work in Belgium and Brussels mobility required.




Languages

Fluency in English is required, along with full professional proficiency in either French or Dutch.

Trilingual EN/FR/NL is a strong asset.




What makes you stand out?

Production Generative AI deployments you can point to.
A Master's/PhD in CS, mathematics, physics, or engineering.
Azure AI Engineer or Azure Data Scientist Associate certification.
Public work: open source, papers, conference talks.
Experience in a regulated vertical: finance, pharma, public sector.




What Exsolvæ Offer:

Transparent compensation, discussed from the first interview, plus our Solver Incentive Program.
Full focus on the work, no internal reporting overhead.
Engagements we can deliver well.
Real growth paths and time to publish or speak.




What this means for you:

Work on production Artificial Intelligence (RAG, agents and MCP). For clients serious about deploying it.
A €2,000 annual Knowledge & Networking budget for certifications and conferences.
A GenAI workspace and current tools from day one, through our OpenAI partnership.
Titles earned through demonstrated work.




Feel like a Solver? Reach out.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Job Description:




Pre-Sales & Discovery

Partner with the Sales Team on client discovery calls to provide technical depth, assess solution fit, and scope Data-as-a-Service opportunities
Develop and present tailored technical assets including specifications, data dictionaries, sample datasets, and client-specific demonstrations to illustrate feasibility and value
Define project scope and success criteria in collaboration with customer stakeholders and internal delivery teams, ensuring alignment on technical requirements and capacity
Design and execute calibration processes including baseline batches, benchmark reports, and evaluation frameworks that establish measurable project success metrics

Project Execution & Delivery

Build and deploy evaluators, design and implement quality measurement systems to validate project outputs and ensure deliverables meet client expectations
Generate synthetic datasets by developing or adapting existing pipelines to accelerate client engagements and augment training data
Package and deliver production-grade datasets with standardized formatting, comprehensive documentation, and quality assurance
Configure and build custom applications and off-platform solutions for non-standard or specialized client requirements




Production & Technical Partnership

Define production specifications and workflows, securing technical alignment with client teams to enable seamless go-live transitions
Provide ongoing technical support to Delivery Managers, addressing complex questions, resolving technical blockers, and supporting customer rebuttals
Maintain specification consistency and alignment across customer and internal teams throughout the engagement lifecycle
Identify and document workflow best practices and automation opportunities, collaborating with DaaS Engineering to continuously improve delivery capabilities




Technical Leadership & Innovation

Maintain solution leaderboards and execute custom model benchmarking on existing datasets to demonstrate technical capabilities
Drive continuous improvement of technical assets, evaluation frameworks, and delivery processes to enhance speed, quality, and scalability
Support account growth by identifying upsell and cross-sell opportunities based on technical interactions with client engineering and research teams

Qualifications

7+ years of experience in data science and engineering roles.
Strong practical experience with Python, SQL, and data tooling (e.g., pandas, Plotly, Streamlit, Dash)
Familiarity with LLM-based workflows and applying ML techniques in production contexts
Experience leveraging Backend APIs and interpreting associated technical documentation
Experience developing technical specifications and designing evaluation workflows
Ability to thrive as a technical communicator and work well as a key member of a team
Strong problem-solving mindset, comfortable working on projects that impact real production systems

Preferred Qualifications

Experience working throughout the entire presales to delivery lifecycle.
Experience identifying upsell and cross-sell opportunities based on technical interactions.
Experience configuring and building custom applications for non-standard or specialized client requirements.
Previous experience in a high-impact, customer-facing role partnering with leading labs and enterprises.
Ability to provide ongoing technical support to Delivery Managers, resolving technical blockers and supporting customer rebuttals.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
The Role

You'll be working as a Data Scientist within an innovative health-tech company where you will apply machine learning, statistical modelling, and advanced analytics to develop data-driven solutions that improve healthcare delivery and patient care.




Responsibilities

Develop and deploy predictive models for healthcare applications including patient risk stratification, disease progression prediction, hospital readmission analysis, clinical decision support, patient segmentation, and healthcare resource forecasting.
Design and implement feature engineering and data preprocessing workflows for clinical, operational, patient-generated, electronic health record (EHR), and time-series healthcare data.
Apply statistical techniques and machine learning algorithms to solve complex healthcare challenges, improve patient outcomes, and support evidence-based clinical and operational decision-making.
Build, validate, and evaluate predictive models using appropriate performance metrics, validation methodologies, and experimentation frameworks to ensure accuracy, reliability, and clinical relevance.




Your Profile

2–5+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, Advanced Analytics, or a related field.
Strong programming skills in Python and experience with data science libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, or similar.
Experience developing predictive models and applying statistical analysis to real-world problems.
Strong understanding of data preparation, feature engineering, and model evaluation methodologies.
Proficiency in SQL and working with large-scale datasets.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP is beneficial.




The Offer

Hybrid working setup with offices located in Brussels.
Opportunity to contribute to innovative data science and analytics solutions within a fast-growing fintech environment.
Strong emphasis on technical development, experimentation, and applied machine learning.
Competitive salary package of up to €6,000 depending on experience and interview performance.
Company car or mobility budget included.
13+ ADV days.
Clear pathways for progression into Senior Data Scientist, Lead Data Scientist, or Analytics Leadership positions.
Collaborative and high-performing team focused on building next-generation financial technology solutions.




If this opportunity excites you, apply today or send your CV and a short cover letter to ryan.martin@vividresourcing.com.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist (ML & GenAI)- Remote Contract | Spain, Portugal or Sweden




We're hiring a Data Scientist with a strong ML background and a genuine interest in GenAI to join a global engineering team working on two really interesting internal products.




This is a mid-level role with a July start, running through to September with a high likelihood of extension beyond that. You'll be embedded across both projects, working alongside existing data scientists and contributing to real, production-grade solutions — not just keeping things at PoC stage.




The Projects




The first is a field service scheduling platform — a live, actively developed tool that manages when engineers need to go on-site for maintenance. There's a working live schedule already in place and the team are continuing to build and iterate on it.




The second is a GenAI-powered chatbot that's moving in a more ambitious direction — the team are rebuilding the UI and evolving the product significantly. It's earlier stage, which means more room to shape the direction, with some genuinely interesting GenAI concepts in play.




What You'll Be Doing




Supporting both projects as an embedded Data Scientist, working closely with the existing science team
Developing and deploying ML models including predictive, prescriptive, and generative AI solutions
Integrating LLMs and GenAI capabilities into analytical workflows and production systems
Building and maintaining ETL pipelines on AWS
Analysing complex datasets to surface patterns and insights that drive real decisions
Designing experiments, performing statistical analysis, and validating model performance
Collaborating with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into working data science solutions
Building with production in mind — this team needs someone who can deploy and manage systems, not just experiment




What We're Looking For




Strong ML and data science fundamentals — statistical modelling, supervised/unsupervised learning, feature engineering, hypothesis testing
Hands-on GenAI experience — LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG concepts
Solid Python and SQL skills — these are non-negotiable
ETL pipeline experience, ideally on AWS
Some software engineering background — you're comfortable deploying things and managing systems in a production environment
Experience with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch
Familiarity with MLOps best practices, Docker, Git, and Jupyter
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work with less day-to-day direction
Based in Spain, Portugal, or Sweden




Nice to Have




Experience with data visualisation tools — matplotlib, seaborn, plotly
Knowledge of knowledge graphs and data connectivity
Familiarity with AWS CDK or similar IaC tooling
Experience working within large enterprise or industrial environments




The Details




Fully remote — B2B contract
Start date: beginning of July
Initial contract through to September — high chance of extension
Based in Spain, Portugal, or Sweden

Title: Data Scientist (Python & SQL) - Freelance AI Trainer
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Who We Are

We are a leader in fraud prevention and AML compliance. Our platform uses device intelligence, behavior biometrics, machine learning, and AI to stop fraud before it happens. Today, over 300 banks, retailers, and fintechs worldwide use Sardine to stop identity fraud, payment fraud, account takeovers, and social engineering scams. We have raised $145M from world-class investors, including Andreessen Horowitz, Activant, Visa, Experian, FIS, and Google Ventures.

Our Culture


We have hubs in the Bay Area, NYC, Austin, Toronto, and São Paulo. However, we maintain a remote-first work culture. #WorkFromAnywhere
We hire talented, self-motivated individuals with extreme ownership and high growth orientation.
We value performance and not hours worked. We believe you shouldn't have to miss your family dinner, your kid's school play, friends get-together, or doctor's appointments for the sake of adhering to an arbitrary work schedule.


Location


UK, Germany, Ireland, Spain, Poland, Bulgaria and Lithuania - Remote
From Home / Beach / Mountain / Cafe / Anywhere!
We are a remote-first company with a globally distributed team. So you can find your productive zone and work from there.


About The Role

We're looking for a data-driven professional to help us measure, understand, and improve the performance of our risk strategies — and to stay ahead of evolving fraud threats by designing and deploying data-driven solutions with real-world impact. You'll work directly with clients to understand their unique fraud challenges, rapidly prototype proof-of-concept models, and build scalable, production-ready solutions using machine learning and graph analytics.

You'll also analyze complex datasets, design metrics, build dashboards, and collaborate closely with stakeholders across the business to drive decision-making and optimize outcomes.

This is a hands-on, high-impact role ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of data science, client-facing problem solving, and real-time risk.

What You'll Be Doing


Champion a data-first approach across internal teams and client engagements, promoting clarity and impact
Build and deploy machine learning models to prevent fraud across diverse fintech use cases, from proof-of-concept through to production
Develop and track metrics to measure and monitor the performance of our risk products and the effectiveness of risk management strategies
Conduct in-depth analyses to uncover insights contributing to fraud reduction and higher approval rates for our clients
Work directly with clients to understand their fraud challenges and translate complex data insights into clear, actionable recommendations
Use data and models to support the development of risk mitigation strategies and interventions while preserving and improving the user experience
Create and automate self-serve dashboards leveraging BI tools
Collaborate with engineering to scale models into production, optimize performance, and support data instrumentation
Partner with cross-functional teams (Business, Product, and Engineering) to translate business requirements into data-driven solutions


What You'll Need


7+ years of experience in data science, quantitative modeling, or a data-focused role (product analytics, business analytics) with demonstrated high impact in fraud or risk contexts
Strong hands-on experience with Python/R and SQL is essential, with Spark being a nice to have
Expertise in BI tools such as Tableau, Sigma, or Metabase
Proven ability to structure and analyze complex data using techniques like EDA and cohort analysis, and communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences, including clients
Sharp critical thinking and creative problem-solving skills with a bias toward action
Proficiency in defining, tracking, and communicating performance metrics


Benefits We Offer


Generous compensation in cash and equity
Early exercise for all options, including pre-vested
Work from anywhere: Remote-first Culture
Flexible paid time off and Year-end break
Health insurance, dental, and vision coverage for employees and dependents - US and Canada specific
4% matching in 401k / RRSP - US and Canada specific
MacBook Pro delivered to your door
One-time stipend to set up a home office — desk, chair, screen, etc.
Monthly meal stipend
Monthly social meet-up stipend
Annual health and wellness stipend
Annual Learning stipend


Join a fast-growing company with world-class professionals from around the world. If you are seeking a meaningful career, you found the right place, and we would love to hear from you.

To learn more about how we process your personal information and your rights in regards to your personal information as an applicant and Sardine employee, please visit our Applicant and Worker Privacy Notice.

Title: ML & GenAI Team Lead (Sagacify)
Description:
Craftzing and Sagacify are looking for an ML & GenAI Team Lead who is equally at home in a pull request as in a people conversation. Someone who does not see leadership and technical depth as a trade-off, but as two things that make each other better.

Do you get energy from building high-performing teams while staying close to the code and the architecture? Then you will probably feel right at home here.

In this role you help build and steer a team of ML and AI Engineers at Sagacify, part of Craftzing, while staying an active contributor on client projects that actually ship. You sit at the intersection of teams, offices and disciplines - reporting to the Head of ML at Sagacify and collaborating closely with the Craftzing delivery organisation. In practice, roughly 70% of your time goes to hands-on delivery and 30% to leading the team.

This is not a role where you hand the technical work off to your team. You stay in the code, you stay in the architecture — and you also grow the people around you.

What you'll do

Team leadership


Lead, mentor and grow a team of five ML and AI Engineers
Set and enforce quality standards, delivery frameworks and best practices
Foster a culture of continuous learning, innovation and knowledge sharing
Own key client relationships, ensuring satisfaction and long-term trust



AI Engineering


Architect end-to-end AI applications orchestrating LLMs, vector databases and APIs
Design and build autonomous agents and conversational systems
Engineer and optimise RAG pipelines connecting enterprise data to LLMs
Perform prompt and context engineering to improve output quality and reduce hallucinations
Evaluate system quality through generative AI metrics and production monitoring
Deploy and scale solutions with focus on latency, security and cost efficiency



MLOps


Design and maintain CI/CD pipelines and automated testing for model deployment
Monitor production models across drift, quality metrics and operational costs
Ensure versioning, reproducibility and safe rollout of models and AI services
Collaborate with DevOps and infrastructure teams for smooth delivery



What you'll work with

Core technologies


Python
OpenAI API, Hugging Face Transformers
LangChain, LangGraph, Langfuse
FastAPI or Flask, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines
Cloud GPU
AWS (S3, SQS, IAM, RDS, etc.)
Cursor



Bonus points for experience with :


TypeScript
Azure
AI-native engineering workflows: hands-on experience using AI coding agents and AI-assisted development environments (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Copilot, etc.), including context engineering, subagent orchestra…



You do not need to master every tool on this list. But you know the fundamentals cold, you pick things up fast, and you are honest about where your gaps are.

Who we're looking for


You have 5 to 9+ years of experience, including at least 2 to 3 years in a tech lead role in ML and AI projects
You have excellent communication skills and are comfortable presenting to C-level stakeholders and facilitating workshops
You have strong organisational skills and can manage multiple workstreams simultaneously
You know how to coach and grow talent while remaining individually impactful on delivery
You are curious and genuinely passionate about AI, with the ability to evaluate and adopt new tools quickly
You communicate fluently in Dutch and English; French is a strong plus



Don't worry if you don't tick every single box - what matters most is the right mindset and a drive to keep learning.

Why you'll love working here

Sagacify is part of Craftzing, and together we build AI solutions that create lasting impact for our clients. You will be part of a team of enthusiasts who love to learn, continuously develop new skills and fast-track our customers' AI journey across various industries.

At Craftzing and Sagacify, you get the trust and space to do your best work - in a human-sized environment with real team spirit, flexible working and room to take ownership.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Full Stack Data Scientist / ML Engineer

Freelance - contract opportunity




We are looking for a Full Stack Data Scientist / ML Engineer to support a global Medical Device business on their AI journey.




You will play a pivotal role in advancing our Advanced Analytics capabilities, acting as both a technical leader and a trusted partner to business stakeholders across the organisation.




This is a high-impact position for someone who takes genuine ownership, leads by example, and is passionate about embedding data science best practices at scale.




Responsibitlies

• Partner with stakeholders across the organisation to understand business objectives and co-design data-driven solutions

• Work closely with Analytics Product Owners and the Cloud/Engineering team to deliver Data Science and ML workstreams on time, within budget, and to quality standards

• Lead and facilitate ideation and scoping sessions with business stakeholders to define the data needed to address specific challenges

• Champion best practices, standards, and methodologies across your Advanced Analytics capability area, ensuring consistently high-quality data science output

• Articulate the value of your domain (e.g., AI/ML) to the broader organisation, shaping decision-making and becoming a go-to advisor for key business stakeholders

• Evaluate and recommend new technologies and ML techniques that improve efficiency and accelerate delivery

• Foster a culture of continuous learning, knowledge sharing, and professional growth within the team

• Own the end-to-end documentation, demos, and presentations required to communicate and validate your solutions










Requirements:

• Azure AI Services — AI Foundry, OpenAI, AI Search, Document Intelligence

• Container Apps, Container App Environments, and Container Registries

• Application Gateway, Key Vault, Managed Identities

• Storage Accounts and Virtual Networks

• Python — production-grade code, with strong experience in LangChain, Pydantic, and FastAPI

• ETL development, including parallel computing approaches

• Generative AI — Prompt Engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and GenAI application architecture

• Terraform (Infrastructure as Code)

• Docker

• Code versioning via Azure Repos

• CI/CD pipeline development using Azure Pipelines

• Linting, unit testing, and adherence to software development best practices

• Industry experience within a highly regulated environment, MedTech, Life Sciences, Food




Nice to Have

• React development experience, including TypeScript, REST/GraphQL, state management, UI/UX principles, HTML/CSS, and caching strategies

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
The Role

We’re partnering with an innovative ClimateTech company building intelligent AI-driven solutions that improve environmental sustainability, operational efficiency, and resource optimization. They are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to develop and deploy scalable ML solutions for real-world environmental and industrial challenges.




What You’ll Be Doing

Develop and deploy machine learning models for sustainability-focused applications such as energy optimization, predictive maintenance, emissions tracking, anomaly detection, and environmental analytics
Create scalable ML infrastructure and pipelines for training, evaluation, monitoring, and real-time model serving across industrial and climate-focused platforms
Design data preprocessing and feature engineering workflows for complex datasets including IoT, sensor, geospatial, and time-series data
Help build intelligent monitoring and optimization systems leveraging technologies such as edge devices, remote sensing, connected infrastructure, and live sensor networks
Contribute to data-driven solutions that improve operational efficiency, reduce environmental impact, and support sustainability initiatives




What They’re Looking For

2–6+ years of experience in Machine Learning Engineering or similar AI-focused roles
Strong proficiency in Python
Hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Scikit-learn
Track record of building, deploying, and maintaining ML systems in production
Experience working with environmental, industrial, energy, IoT, mobility, or geospatial datasets is considered a strong plus
Understanding of data engineering practices including ETL/ELT pipelines, streaming systems, and large-scale time-series data processing
Experience with cloud infrastructure such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Interest in applying AI to sustainability, clean technology, smart infrastructure, or environmental intelligence challenges




What’s on Offer

Hybrid working model with offices located in Aalst
Opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge ClimateTech solutions with tangible environmental impact
Collaborative environment with a strong emphasis on innovation, experimentation, and applied AI
Competitive salary package up to €6,000/month depending on experience
Company car or flexible mobility budget
13+ additional ADV leave days
Clear career progression opportunities toward senior and leadership roles
Mission-driven culture focused on building technology for a more sustainable future




If this opportunity excites you, apply today or send your CV and a short cover letter to ryan.martin@vividresourcing.com.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Voor het Agentschap Vlaamse Dienst voor Arbeidsbemiddeling en Beroepsopleiding (VDAB), is Vlaanderen connect op zoek naar een:

Senior Data Scientist

Contract onbepaalde duur

Brussel

///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

Bij VDAB geloven we dat data en artificiële intelligentie de motor zijn van een moderne arbeidsmarkt. VDAB heeft daarom een matuur AI Center of Excellence (“AI CoE”) uitgebouwd, dat een centrale rol speelt in het vormgeven en begeleiden van deze digitale evolutie.

Voor dit AI CoE zoekt VDAB een ervaren Data Scientist, die leiding en coaching geeft aan het team van Data Scientists, en die de fundamenten van het AI Center of Excellence mee bewaakt en uitbouwt.










Waarom voor deze functie kiezen?

VDAB is een voortrekker in het toepassen van artificiële intelligentie binnen de publieke sector en wil deze positie in de komende jaren verder versterken.

Je krijgt de kans om te werken in een vooruitstrevende en innovatieve omgeving die niet alleen technisch uitdagend is, maar je kan ook een directe maatschappelijke meerwaarde creëren. De oplossingen die we samen realiseren, ondersteunen burgers in elke fase van hun loopbaan en dragen tastbaar bij aan een inclusieve, efficiënte en toekomstgerichte arbeidsmarkt.

Kortom, deze rol biedt een unieke combinatie van technologische innovatie, strategische impact en maatschappelijke relevantie.







Jouw verantwoordelijkheden

Je bent succesvol in deze rol wanneer je:

We verwachten van de kandidaat dat hij/zij zelfstandig van begin tot einde een AI model kan opzetten. Dit dus gaande van het samenstellen van de data, data analyse, kiezen van het correcte model, evaluatie van de output en ondersteuning bij het in productie zetten van dit model. Daarnaast verwachten we dat de kandidaat ook in staat is om zijn/haar keuzes uit te leggen aan een publiek met niet-technische achtergrond. Dit zowel op basis van een presentatie als op basis van een rapport.

Daarnaast verwachten we dat je leiding en coaching geeft aan het team van Data Scientists waarbij je :

* een sterke vertrouwensband opbouwt met de AI Data Scientists

* hen ondersteunt als coach maar ook uitdaagt om het beste uit zichzelf te halen in het belang van de organisatiedoelen

* een volledig overzicht hebt van de huidige AI portfolio en de lopende AI projecten, en de kritieke pijnpunten op vlak van stabiliteit en onderhoudbaarheid kent

* duidelijke afspraken maakt over o.a. code reviews, testing (unit/integratie) en documentatievereisten voor alle nieuwe AI-projecten, in het kader van de AI Governance van VDAB

* bijdraagt aan een geïntegreerde en performante samenwerking van Projectleiders, Data Scientists en Data Engineers

* bijdraagt aan de strategie en visie van het AI Center of Excellence

* bijdraagt aan een goede werking en goede relaties met andere teams zowel binnen als buiten I&T







De 3 belangrijkste verantwoordelijkheden :

Je stuurt en coacht een performant team van Data Scientists. Je zorgt daarbij voor een cultuur van continue verbetering, kennisdeling en technologische innovatie.
Je vertaalt business problemen naar ML/AI oplossingen en je ontwikkelt mee AI/ML modellen van experiment tot productie.
Je bewaakt mee de roadmap en de stabiliteit van de AI-applicaties







Wat zijn de belangrijkste taken?

Ontwikkelen van AI/ML/LLM modellen
Coachen en challengen van de meest geschikte oplossingen (bv ML/ LLM) gegeven de uitdaging
Delen van kennis en inzichten van ontwikkelde modellen







Wat zijn je technische vaardigheden ?







Master of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
Diepgaande kennis van statistiek/probabiliteitstheorie
Diepgaande kennis van Machine Learning Algoritmes.
Uitgebreide ervaring met Python en (Py)Spark
Uitgebreide ervaring met GIT of andere code repositories
Uitgebreide ervaring met generatieve AI toepassingen en modellen
Ervaring met NLP is een pluspunt
Ervaring met Deep Learning is een pluspunt
Ervaring met het in productie zetten van ML oplossingen is een pluspunt
Op de hoogte zijn van de laatste evoluties op het vlak van AI




Welke zijn je persoonlijkheid vaardigheden ?

* Teamplayer - gericht op samenwerking zowel met business teams als technische teams

* People management en ervaring met coachend leiderschap

* Resultaatgericht

* Kritische en oplossingsgerichte denker

* Bewezen ervaring met stakeholder management

* Bewezen ervaring in constructief omgaan met conflicterende visies

* Ervaring met innovatie




Waar ga je werken ?

Je werkt 2 dagen in Brussel, op wandelafstand van het station Brussel Noord.




Jouw toekomstig team

Het team waarvoor je verantwoordelijk bent, bestaat momenteel uit 10 teamleden, waar we de rollen van junior, medior en senior Data Scientist terugvinden. Je werkt nauw samen met een Manager AI Engineering en de Portfolio Manager die het AI Portfolio beheert en de Project Managers aanstuurt. En uiteraard met de Manager van het AI Center of Excellence, aan wie je rapporteert.




Wat zijn typische projecten van deze functie/afdeling?




Typische projecten ontstaan vanuit een idee (ter ondersteuning van burger, werkgever of medewerkers van VDAB) afkomstig van binnen in het team of van business. Hierbij ligt de focus altijd op kwaliteits- en/of efficiëntieverhoging. We starten hier rond een experiment om om te kijken welke meerwaarde we kunnen bieden met behulp van data en AI. Indien dit positief verloopt, wordt het model in meer detail uitgewerkt en wordt er een testbaar prototype ontwikkeld. De volgende stap is dan het in productie plaatsen van de applicatie.

Vlaanderen connect biedt een contract van 38 uur voor onbepaalde duur en een competitief salarispakket met:

Een aantrekkelijke bruto verloning
Een bonussysteem op basis van vast te leggen doelstellingen
28 dagen verlof en extra wettelijke feestdagen (11/07, 02/11 en 15/11).

HTML:
Voor het Agentschap Vlaamse Dienst voor Arbeidsbemiddeling en Beroepsopleiding (VDAB), is Vlaanderen connect op zoek naar een:

Senior Data Scientist

Contract onbepaalde duur

Brussel

///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

Bij VDAB geloven we dat data en artificiële intelligentie de motor zijn van een moderne arbeidsmarkt. VDAB heeft daarom een matuur AI Center of Excellence (“AI CoE”) uitgebouwd, dat een centrale rol speelt in het vormgeven en begeleiden van deze digitale evolutie.

Voor dit AI CoE zoekt VDAB een ervaren Data Scientist, die leiding en coaching geeft aan het team van Data Scientists, en die de fundamenten van het AI Center of Excellence mee bewaakt en uitbouwt.




Waarom voor deze functie kiezen?

VDAB is een voortrekker in het toepassen van artificiële intelligentie binnen de publieke sector en wil deze positie in de komende jaren verder versterken.

Je krijgt de kans om te werken in een vooruitstrevende en innovatieve omgeving die niet alleen technisch uitdagend is, maar je kan ook een directe maatschappelijke meerwaarde creëren. De oplossingen die we samen realiseren, ondersteunen burgers in elke fase van hun loopbaan en dragen tastbaar bij aan een inclusieve, efficiënte en toekomstgerichte arbeidsmarkt.

Kortom, deze rol biedt een unieke combinatie van technologische innovatie, strategische impact en maatschappelijke relevantie.



Jouw verantwoordelijkheden

Je bent succesvol in deze rol wanneer je:

We verwachten van de kandidaat dat hij/zij zelfstandig van begin tot einde een AI model kan opzetten. Dit dus gaande van het samenstellen van de data, data analyse, kiezen van het correcte model, evaluatie van de output en ondersteuning bij het in productie zetten van dit model. Daarnaast verwachten we dat de kandidaat ook in staat is om zijn/haar keuzes uit te leggen aan een publiek met niet-technische achtergrond. Dit zowel op basis van een presentatie als op basis van een rapport.

Daarnaast verwachten we dat je leiding en coaching geeft aan het team van Data Scientists waarbij je :

* een sterke vertrouwensband opbouwt met de AI Data Scientists

* hen ondersteunt als coach maar ook uitdaagt om het beste uit zichzelf te halen in het belang van de organisatiedoelen

* een volledig overzicht hebt van de huidige AI portfolio en de lopende AI projecten, en de kritieke pijnpunten op vlak van stabiliteit en onderhoudbaarheid kent

* duidelijke afspraken maakt over o.a. code reviews, testing (unit/integratie) en documentatievereisten voor alle nieuwe AI-projecten, in het kader van de AI Governance van VDAB

* bijdraagt aan een geïntegreerde en performante samenwerking van Projectleiders, Data Scientists en Data Engineers

* bijdraagt aan de strategie en visie van het AI Center of Excellence

* bijdraagt aan een goede werking en goede relaties met andere teams zowel binnen als buiten I&T



De 3 belangrijkste verantwoordelijkheden :

  • Je stuurt en coacht een performant team van Data Scientists. Je zorgt daarbij voor een cultuur van continue verbetering, kennisdeling en technologische innovatie.
  • Je vertaalt business problemen naar ML/AI oplossingen en je ontwikkelt mee AI/ML modellen van experiment tot productie.
  • Je bewaakt mee de roadmap en de stabiliteit van de AI-applicaties

  • Ontwikkelen van AI/ML/LLM modellen

Description:
Ben jij klaar om je Data Science carrière naar een hoger niveau te tillen? Bij Randstad Digital zoeken we een technische expert die ons helpt in het ontwikkelen van voorspellende modellen, het ontsluiten van inzichten uit complexe datasets en het implementeren van geavanceerde AI-oplossingen.

Jouw uitdaging

• Je past geavanceerde technieken toe op het gebied van NLP en LLM's om waarde te halen uit ongestructureerde data en processen te automatiseren.

• Je voert diepgaande analyses uit en bouwt modellen die trends voorspellen en de besluitvorming binnen de organisatie optimaliseren.
