Key challenges facing the successful candidate for the first three-five years will include:

For the Head, Russia Policy Coordination Section:

  • Managing the Russia Policy Coordination team on a busy set of files using sound management principles.
  • Taking account of the evolving security situation in the Euro-Atlantic area in the framework of NATO's policy towards Russia.
  • Providing oversight for the policy work on Russia.


For the Head, Policy East Section:

  • Managing the Policy East Section on a busy set of files using sound management principles.
  • Shaping NATO’s policy towards partners in the area of responsibility while taking account of the evolving security situation in the Euro-Atlantic area.
  • Providing oversight for the cooperation framework between NATO and Ukraine.
  • Candidates applying ONLY to the Head Russia Policy Coordination Section:


In addition to the application form, candidates are expected to submit a Word or pdf document, maximum of 2 A4 pages, summarising their views on the key (and possible other) challenges facing the Russia Policy Coordination Section, and how they would address them if selected for the position.

  • Candidates applying ONLY to the Head, Policy East Section:


In addition to the application form, candidates are expected to submit a Word or pdf document, maximum of 2 A4 pages, summarising their views on the key (and possible other) challenges facing the Policy East Section, and how they would address them if selected for the position.

  • Candidates applying to BOTH the Head Russia Policy Coordination Section & Head, Policy East Section:


In addition to the application form, candidates are expected to submit a Word or pdf document, maximum of 2 A4 pages, summarising their views on the below key challenges, and how they would address them if selected for either position:

  • Managing the team on a busy set of files using sound management principles.
  • Shaping NATO’s policy towards partners in the area of responsibility and towards Russia while taking account of the evolving security situation in the Euro-Atlantic area.
  • Providing oversight for the policy work on Russia AND the cooperation framework between NATO and Ukraine.
  • QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE


The incumbent must:

  • possess a university degree or equivalent qualification in the field of political science, public or international relations or another relevant discipline;
  • have at least 10 years of relevant professional experience, out of which at least 5 years’ experience in managing and leading a team;
  • have experience of work dealing with Russia (specific for Head Russia Policy Coordination Section – Post PASP0150)
  • demonstrate a strong understanding of security issues in the Euro-Atlantic area;
  • have experience in programme/project management;
  • possess the following minimum levels of NATO’s official languages (English/French): V (“Advanced”) in one; I (“Beginner”) in the other.


The following would be considered an advantage:

  • working knowledge of Russian (knowledge of Ukrainian would be an asset for Head, Policy East Section – Post PASP0024)
  • experience of work in/with one of the countries under the responsibility of the Section (specific for Head, Policy East Section – Post PASP0024)
  • diplomatic experience in the foreign service of a member country or in an international organisation.
  • MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES


Refer to the links to the different PDs, here below:

  • The Russia Policy Coordination Section (G22) (PASP0150) - Click here for post description.
  • The Policy East Section (G22) (PASP0024) - Click here for post description.
  • INTERRELATIONSHIPS


Reporting Line: The incumbent reports to the Director of the Partnerships and Global Affairs Directorate.

Interactions: The incumbent interacts with managers and senior officers throughout the IS, International Military Staff, allied delegations and partner missions and represents the Section and Directorate on a frequent basis. The incumbent also engages with international organisations and NGOs.

The Russia Policy Coordination Section (G22) (PASP0024):

  • Direct Reports: 3
  • Indirect reports: N/A


The Policy East Section (G22) (PASP0024):

  • Direct Reports: 8
  • Indirect reports: 4
  • COMPETENCIES


The incumbent must demonstrate:

  • Achievement: Sets and works to meet challenging goals;
  • Analytical Thinking: Sees multiple relationships;
  • Change Leadership: Personally leads change;
  • Impact and Influence: Uses complex influence strategies;
  • Initiative: Plans and acts up to a year ahead;
  • Organisational Awareness: Understands underlying issues;
  • Teamwork: Works to build commitment.
  • CONTRACT


Contract to be offered to the successful applicant (if non-seconded): Definite duration contract of three years; possibility of renewal for up to three years, during which the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite duration contract.

Contract clause applicable:

In accordance with the contract policy, this is a post in which turnover is desirable for political reasons in order to be able to accommodate the Organisation's need to carry out its tasks as mandated by the Nations in a changing environment, for example by maintaining the flexibility necessary to shape the Organisation's skills profile, and to ensure appropriate international diversity.

The maximum period of service foreseen in this post is 6 years. The successful applicant will be offered a 3-year definite duration contract, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years. However, according to the procedure described in the contract policy the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite contract during the period of renewal and no later than one year before the end of contract.

If the successful applicant is seconded from the national administration of one of NATO’s member States, a 3-year definite duration contract will be offered, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years subject also to the agreement of the national authority concerned. The maximum period of service in the post as a seconded staff member is six years.

Serving staff will be offered a contract in accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Régulations.

  • USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS


Please note that we can only accept applications from nationals of NATO member countries. Applications must be submitted using e-recruitment system, as applicable:

  • For NATO civilian staff members only: please apply via the internal recruitment portal ( link );
  • For all other applications: www.nato.int/recruitment


Before you apply to any position, we encourage you to click here and watch our video providing 6 tips to prepare you for your application and recruitment process.

Do you have questions on the application process in the system and not sure how to proceed? Click here for a video containing the information you need to successfully submit your application on time.

When submitting your application, please ensure that your Taleo Candidate Profile is updated and that your CV is correctly uploaded in the Taleo attachments section.

Please note that the competition for this post is provisionally scheduled as follows:

Pre-selection testing: Second half of July 2026;

Final selection: End of August / Beginning of September 2026, in Brussels, Belgium.

More information about the recruitment process and conditions of employment, can be found at our website (http://www.nato.int/cps/en/natolive/recruit-hq-e.htm)

Appointment will be subject to receipt of a security clearance (provided by the national Authorities of the selected candidate), approval of the candidate’s medical file by the NATO Medical Adviser, verification of your study(ies) and work experience, and the successful completion of the accreditation and notification process by the relevant authorities.

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection prepared, in whole or in part, by means of reference documents without proper quotes (plagiarism), or any tools available on internet, including but not limited to translation facilities, or generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such tools will be rejected without further consideration, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.

  • ADDITIONAL INFORMATION


NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion, and strives to provide equal access to employment, advancement and retention, independent of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religion or belief, cultural background, sexual orientation, and disability. NATO welcomes applications of nationals from all member Nations, and strongly encourages women to apply.

NATO is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible working environment, where all candidates living with disabilities can fully participate in the recruitment and selection process. If you require reasonable accommodation, please inform us during your selection process.

Candidates will be required to provide documented medical evidence to support their request for accommodation.

Building Integrity is a key element of NATO’s core tasks. As an employer, NATO values commitment to the principles of integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector. Selected candidates are expected to be role models of integrity, and to promote good governance through ongoing efforts in their work.

Due to the broad interest in NATO and the large number of potential candidates, telephone or e-mail enquiries cannot be dealt with.

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

For information about the NATO Single Salary Scale (Grading, Allowances, etc.) please visit our website . Detailed data is available under the Salary and Benefits tab.

NATO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. For more info please click here .

Title: Data Scientist - Banking
Description:
Data Scientist – Financial Services

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international organization headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with over 350 consultants dedicated to driving business transformation through innovative technology.

Our Financial Services (FS) practice includes 100+ specialists helping banks and financial institutions adapt to evolving client expectations, leverage cutting-edge technologies, and navigate increasingly complex regulatory environments.

At NORRIQ, we believe that people are our greatest asset. We foster a culture of innovation, growth, and excellence, offering tailored training, clear career paths, and a strong sense of community. Join us on this exciting journey and become a valued member of the NORRIQ family — connected for life!

The Opportunity

NORRIQ Financial Services is looking for a Data Scientist to accelerate our data analytics, machine learning, and generative AI initiatives. You will work with leading financial institutions to design, build, and deliver data-driven and AI-powered solutions that create tangible business impact.

Roles & Responsibilities

Client-Facing Responsibilities


Design and implement machine learning and generative AI solutions for complex financial use cases.
Conduct exploratory data analysis and build insightful dashboards or visualizations.
Present analytical findings and actionable recommendations to senior management and stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Data Engineers, Business Analysts, Developers) to industrialize AI solutions.
Provide technical guidance and advisory on data science and AI best practices.


Technical Responsibilities


Develop and maintain AI and ML models using Python, scikit-learn, and deep learning frameworks.
Apply a wide range of analytical methodologies, including:
Generative AI techniques (Prompt Engineering, RAG, Fine-tuning, Speech-to-Text, Agentic Applications, Image Generation & Interpretation)
Predictive AI and classical machine learning approaches.
Contribute to production-grade AI applications using tools such as GitLab, DVC, and MLOps frameworks.
Ensure reproducibility, version control, and performance optimization across models and pipelines.
Create and maintain Jupyter notebooks for experimentation and documentation.

Internal Contributions


Contribute to NORRIQ’s AI & Data Science Center of Excellence through knowledge sharing and internal projects.
Support proposal writing and solution architecture for client engagements.
Stay at the forefront of developments in data science, AI, and GenAI frameworks (LangChain, Haystack, etc.).


Our Data Scientist Must Have


Fluency in English, and Dutch or French, with at least passive knowledge of the other
Belgian residency
At least few years of experience in a similar role.


What NORRIQ Offers You


Be part of a fast-growing, international organization with a unique and people-centered culture
Enjoy a highly competitive compensation package, including a company car, iPhone, and additional allowances
Access extensive training programs in Belgium and Denmark, focusing on both technical expertise and soft skills
Work with a dynamic and diverse team of experienced professionals and young talent in a collaborative environment within the financial services sector


Join NORRIQ to elevate your career, contribute to transformative projects, and make a lasting impact in the financial services industry!

Title: Werfleider - tijdelijke opdracht
HTML:
Vanhout is een klasse 8 aannemer met hoofdkantoor in Geel. Onze bouwprojecten zijn verspreid over gans Vlaanderen. Wij zijn actief in een brede waaier van bouwactiviteiten: zorgprojecten, kantoorgebouwen, utilitaire en residentiële projecten, sportinfrastructuur zoals zwembaden en voetbalstadia en industriële productie-eenheden.

Vanhout wil zich blijvend profileren als een betrouwbare en competente aannemer, waarbij tevredenheid van alle stakeholders, innovatie en kwaliteit voorop staan. Vanhout ambieert om meer te zijn dan een aannemer. Samenwerking in TEAM is één van onze uitgangspunten en dit doen we door RESPECT te hebben voor elkaar en in alle OPENHEID met elkaar om te gaan. We investeren in de groei van onze medewerkers zodat zij hun TALENT kunnen ontplooien. Dit alles draagt bij tot onze positieve SFEER waar de Vanhout Vibes ook deel van uitmaken. We willen exce!!eren in onze dienstverlening naar klanten. We zijn dan ook TROTS! om de helm van Vanhout te dragen!



Vacatureomschrijving

Als werfleider sta je in voor het aansturen, coördineren, opvolgen en bijsturen van alle werkzaamheden voor het realiseren van een project, en dit vanaf de voorbereiding van de werf tot en met de definitieve oplevering van het gebouw.

  • Je bent verantwoordelijk voor de werfopvolging, ontwikkeling en planning van een gedeelte van of een volledige werf (afhankelijk van de grootte);
  • Je bewaakt de uitvoeringsplanning en overlegt frequent met de projectleider m.b.t. afwijkingen;
  • Je controleert de kwaliteit en de maatvoering van de uitgevoerde werken;
  • Je zorgt ervoor dat iedereen op de werf alle richtlijnen m.b.t. kwaliteit, veiligheid, gezondheid en milieu correct opvolgt;
  • Je organiseert en coördineert de logistiek, materialen en mankracht (+ opvolging);
  • Je stuurt de dagelijkse uitvoering aan van het project;
  • Je selecteert en volgt de onderaannemers op;
  • Je onderhoudt het contact met de klanten en diens vertegenwoordigers;
  • Je woont belangrijke werfvergaderingen bij en verzorgt de nodige rapportering;

Functie-eisen

  • Je hebt een Bachelor of Master diploma in bouwkunde;
  • Je beschikt over 4-7 jaar ervaring in de uitvoering van soortgelijke bouwprojecten.
  • Kenmerkend zijn jouw coördinatie- en organisatievaardigheden, je communicatieskills, en stressbestendigheid.
  • Je bent sterk organisatorisch ingesteld.
  • Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands.
  • Je bent mee onze (T)ROTS! in de branding om onze visie en waarden binnen het bedrijf en buiten het bedrijf te communiceren.

Aanvullende informatie

Een carrière bij Vanhout is veel meer dan enkel een job. Deel uitmaken van de Groep Vanhout is deel uitmaken van een zeer dynamische en ondernemende organisatie met ambitieuze en innovatieve doelen. We zijn op zoek naar mensen met verschillende persoonlijkheden, denkwijzen en met verschillende achtergronden. We zoeken mensen met passie voor de bouw en die mee willen bouwen aan de wereld van morgen!

Werken bij Vanhout houdt in dat je veel interessante voordelen krijgt:

  • Een uitdagende functie bij een gevestigde waarde in de bouwwereld.
  • Een logistieke dienst met lasatelier en eigen ketenpark.
  • Je komt terecht in een organisatie waar unieke Vanhout Vibes heersen, met andere woorden een fijne werkplek waar we samen hard werken om tot de beste resultaten te komen, maar waar ook plaats is voor écht plezier (pingpongtornooi, inzetten van het bouwverlof, teambuildingactiviteiten, …).
  • Een inspirerende en open werkomgeving waar oog is voor jouw verdere technische ontwikkeling en jouw people management skills d.m.v. opleidingen.
  • Een aantrekkelijk salaris.

Is deze vacature je op het lijf geschreven? Aarzel dan niet, stuur ons jouw CV en motivatiebrief (PDF of Word document) en laat van je horen.

Solliciteren kan via jobs@vanhout.be t.a.v. Evelien Baert, HR Recruiter.

Title: Data Coordinator
Description:
Primary Location

Belgium-Brussels

NATO Body

NATO International Staff (NATO IS)

Schedule

Full-time

Application Deadline

14-Jul-2026, 5:59:00 PM

Salary (Pay Basis)

4,738.84

Grade

NATO Grade G10-G12


SUMMARY



The Defence Industry, Innovation and Armaments Division (D2IA) leads on the rapid development and delivery of cutting-edge and interoperable defence capabilities, working with innovation ecosystems and industry across the Alliance to meet NATO’s deterrence and defence needs.

The Data Coordinator will support all Air Section’s activities ensuring an efficient handling of a wide range of support duties of an administrative and organisational nature. In particular, the incumbent will assist the Section’s Head in the execution of the administrative matters of the Aviation Committee and airworthiness tasks with the direct responsibility for population, maintenance, and refinement of an airworthiness database.


QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE



The incumbent must:


possess higher secondary education qualification with 6 years post-related experience, OR upper vocational training/ post-secondary degree in a discipline relevant for this position with 3 years post-related experience of administrative or organisational work;
have in-depth knowledge of office management and information technology systems, software applications and packages, in particular the Microsoft Office suite, Share Point or similar;
possess the following minimum levels of NATO’s official languages (English/French): V (“Advanced”) in one; I (“Beginner”) in the other.



The following would be considered an advantage:


experience in managing systems engineering processes;
experience in aviation related industry.
MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES



Knowledge Management: Maintain relevant information required for various tasks and projects. Assist with the administration and organisational work for the Aviation Committee and its substructures.

Expertise Development: Respond to questions related to airworthiness data and records. Implement, maintain and validate the relevant airworthiness data and establish dashboards to support the work of the section. Proactively develop skills in areas such as planning, organising, and the use of new technology.

Project Management: Contribute to the development and implementation of processes and procedures for the collective recognition and oversight of airworthiness authorities of NATO Nations and Partners, in execution of the provisions of the NATO airworthiness policy. Contribute to projects within the section, as requested.

Perform any other related duty as assigned.


INTERRELATIONSHIPS



Reporting Line: The incumbent reports to the Section Head.

Interactions: The incumbent maintains close working relationships with other staff across the Directorate, NATO Divisions, national delegations and international organisations as required.

Direct reports: N/a

Indirect reports: N/a


COMPETENCIES



The incumbent must demonstrate:


Achievement: works to meet standards.
Analytical Thinking: sees basic relationships.
Clarity and Accuracy: checks own work.
Conceptual Thinking: sees patterns base on life/work experience.
Customer Service Orientation: takes personal responsibility for correcting problems.
Empathy: reads non-verbal cues and understands meanings.
Impact and Influence: takes multiple actions to persuade.
Initiative: is decisive in a time-sensitive situation.
Teamwork: cooperates.
CONTRACT



Contract to be offered to the successful applicant (if non-seconded): Definite duration contract of three years’ duration; possibility of an indefinite duration contract thereafter.

Contract clause applicable:

In accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Regulations, the successful candidate will receive a definite duration contract of three years, which may be followed by an indefinite duration contract.

If the successful applicant is seconded from the national administration of one of NATO’s member States, a 3-year definite duration contract will be offered, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years subject to the agreement of the national authority concerned. The maximum period of service in the post as a seconded staff member is six years.

Serving staff will be offered a contract in accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Regulations.

NOTE: Irrespective of previous qualifications and experience, candidates for twin-graded posts will be appointed at the lower grade. Advancement to the higher grade is not automatic, and will not normally take place during the first three years of service in the post.

Under specific circumstances, serving staff members may be appointed directly to the higher grade, and a period of three years might be reduced by up to twenty four months for external candidates. These circumstances are described in the IS directive on twin-graded posts.


USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS



Please note that we can only accept applications from nationals of NATO member countries. Applications must be submitted using e-recruitment system, as applicable:


For NATO civilian staff members only: please apply via the internal recruitment portal ( link );
For all other applications: www.nato.int/recruitment

Do you have questions on the application process in the system and not sure how to proceed? Click here for a video containing the information you need to successfully submit your application on time.

When submitting your application, please ensure that your Taleo Candidate Profile is updated and that your CV is correctly uploaded in the Taleo attachments section.

More information about the recruitment process and conditions of employment, can be found at our website (http://www.nato.int/cps/en/natolive/recruit-hq-e.htm)

Appointment will be subject to receipt of a security clearance (provided by the national Authorities of the selected candidate), approval of the candidate’s medical file by the NATO Medical Adviser, verification of your study(ies) and work experience, and the successful completion of the accreditation and notification process by the relevant authorities.

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection prepared, in whole or in part, by means of reference documents without proper quotes (plagiarism), or any tools available on internet, including but not limited to translation facilities, or generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such tools will be rejected without further consideration, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION



NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion, and strives to provide equal access to employment, advancement and retention, independent of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religion or belief, cultural background, sexual orientation, and disability. NATO welcomes applications of nationals from all member Nations, and strongly encourages women to apply.

NATO is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible working environment, where all candidates living with disabilities can fully participate in the recruitment and selection process. If you require reasonable accommodation, please inform us during your selection process.

Candidates will be required to provide documented medical evidence to support their request for accommodation.

Building Integrity is a key element of NATO’s core tasks. As an employer, NATO values commitment to the principles of integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector. Selected candidates are expected to be role models of integrity, and to promote good governance through ongoing efforts in their work.

Due to the broad interest in NATO and the large number of potential candidates, telephone or e-mail enquiries cannot be dealt with.

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

Under specific circumstances, serving staff members may be appointed directly to the higher grade, and a period of three years might be reduced by up to twenty four months for external candidates. These circumstances are described in the IS directive on twin-graded posts.

  • USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS


Please note that we can only accept applications from nationals of NATO member countries. Applications must be submitted using e-recruitment system, as applicable:

  • For NATO civilian staff members only: please apply via the internal recruitment portal ( link );
  • For all other applications: www.nato.int/recruitment


Before you apply to any position, we encourage you to click here and watch our video providing 6 tips to prepare you for your application and recruitment process.

Do you have questions on the application process in the system and not sure how to proceed? Click here for a video containing the information you need to successfully submit your application on time.

When submitting your application, please ensure that your Taleo Candidate Profile is updated and that your CV is correctly uploaded in the Taleo attachments section.

More information about the recruitment process and conditions of employment, can be found at our website (http://www.nato.int/cps/en/natolive/recruit-hq-e.htm)

Appointment will be subject to receipt of a security clearance (provided by the national Authorities of the selected candidate), approval of the candidate’s medical file by the NATO Medical Adviser, verification of your study(ies) and work experience, and the successful completion of the accreditation and notification process by the relevant authorities.

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection prepared, in whole or in part, by means of reference documents without proper quotes (plagiarism), or any tools available on internet, including but not limited to translation facilities, or generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such tools will be rejected without further consideration, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.

  • ADDITIONAL INFORMATION


NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion, and strives to provide equal access to employment, advancement and retention, independent of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religion or belief, cultural background, sexual orientation, and disability. NATO welcomes applications of nationals from all member Nations, and strongly encourages women to apply.

NATO is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible working environment, where all candidates living with disabilities can fully participate in the recruitment and selection process. If you require reasonable accommodation, please inform us during your selection process.

Candidates will be required to provide documented medical evidence to support their request for accommodation.

Building Integrity is a key element of NATO’s core tasks. As an employer, NATO values commitment to the principles of integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector. Selected candidates are expected to be role models of integrity, and to promote good governance through ongoing efforts in their work.

Due to the broad interest in NATO and the large number of potential candidates, telephone or e-mail enquiries cannot be dealt with.

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

For information about the NATO Single Salary Scale (Grading, Allowances, etc.) please visit our website . Detailed data is available under the Salary and Benefits tab.

NATO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. For more info please click here .

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
A day in the life of an AI Engineer

As an AI Engineer in the SRE department of Tech BE, you will help shape and evolve agentic Software Delivery Lifecycle (SDLC) workflows, turning GenAI capabilities into reliable, production‑ready engineering solutions.


Developing production‑ready GenAI and agentic solutions by leveraging GitHub GenAI tools (GitHub Copilot, prompts, agent skills, and instruction files) and working with Python‑based services on Google Cloud (GCP) using Vertex AI, FastAPI, LangChain, and Google ADK to deliver real business impact.
Designing intelligent, cloud‑native AI workflows and multi‑agent systems that are reliable, secure, observable, and cost‑efficient in production, integrating GitHub‑based CI/CD, workflows, and automation for scalable operation and evolution.
Collaborating daily with SREs, software engineers, and product partners to translate complex problems into scalable GenAI solutions that improve engineering experience and operational resilience.
Improving AI agent quality through evaluation, testing, monitoring, and feedback loops, while operating services via CI/CD pipelines, GitHub workflows, and Infrastructure‑as‑Code (e.g. Terraform).
Shaping GenAI engineering standards and best practices across ING, helping teams apply responsible AI to strengthen digital services that support customers and society.


How To Succeed

We hire smart people like you for your potential. Our biggest expectation is that you’ll stay curious. Keep learning. Take on responsibility. In return, we’ll back you to develop into an even more awesome version of yourself.

As an AI Engineer, you will be successful in this role if you can:


Turn complex and loosely defined problems into clear, step‑by‑step solutions, knowing when and how GenAI, LLMs, or agentic approaches can meaningfully improve engineering workflows.
Apply a solid software engineering background (from backend, platform, cloud, data, or similar roles) to design, build, and evolve production‑ready solutions using GitHub Copilot, Vertex AI, LangChain, Google ADK, Python, FastAPI, RAG, vectorDB, Terraform, docker and cloud‑native patterns.
Deliver reliable, high‑quality software into production, applying solid engineering principles that may come from backend, platform, data, or cloud roles, with Python, APIs, and cloud‑native thinking as common foundations.
Take ownership of running systems, balancing reliability, security, performance, and cost, and contributing to smooth delivery through modern DevOps practices and GitHub‑based workflows.
Learn and adapt continuously, staying curious and comfortable experimenting with evolving GenAI concepts such as agent orchestration, LLMOps, or AI‑assisted development tools, even if your experience comes from adjacent technologies.
Work effectively with others, combining clear communication, collaboration, and technical judgment to turn AI‑enabled solutions into measurable outcomes that support ING’s purpose and positive societal impact.


As an AI Engineer, you will have the opportunity to:


Grow as an engineer, strengthen your software engineering foundation while developing deep expertise in modern AI and GenAI engineering practices.
Make a tangible impact across ING Tech Belgium, contributing to shared GenAI platforms, standards, and solutions that are reused across teams and tribes.
Continuously learn and evolve, gaining hands‑on experience with new AI engineering technologies, tools, and ways of working as ING scales its agentic and GenAI capabilities.


The team

The SRE Belgium Tribe plays a key role in keeping ING Belgium’s most important digital services reliable, secure, and scalable, supporting essential sales and servicing journeys used daily by customers. Working closely with engineering teams across the bank, SRE focuses on modern engineering practices that strengthen the stability and quality of ING’s technology platforms.

You will join a newly created AI Engineering team within SRE. This team is responsible for building and scaling agentic Generative AI workflows across the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC). Using GitHub‑based technologies, the team develops reusable AI capabilities that help DevOps teams across ING work faster, smarter, and more consistently.

Operating at the intersection of software engineering, reliability, and AI, the team’s mission is to scale Generative AI safely, responsibly, and effectively across ING. By providing standardized platforms and tools, such as GitHub Copilot and VISTA (Google Cloud Platform), the team enables engineers to adopt GenAI with confidence.

Would you like to get to know the department better? Book a coffee with the hiring manager, a team member, or the recruiter. In a second phase you could get to know the job content better via a My Job Your Job.

Title: CMDB Specialist
Description:
CMDB Specialist

Location: Brussels, Belgium (Hybrid – 2 days onsite per week)

Full time role




We are supporting a major organisation undergoing a significant digital transformation programme and are looking for an experienced CMDB Specialist to lead the design, implementation, and optimisation of a centralised Configuration Management Database (CMDB)




As the CMDB Specialist, you will take ownership of the end-to-end CMDB implementation lifecycle, from strategy and design through deployment and continuous improvement.




Required Experience

10+ years of experience designing and implementing CMDB solutions within large enterprise environments.
Strong knowledge of ITSM and ITIL frameworks.
Proven experience leading complex IT transformation or service management projects.
Deep understanding of Change Management processes and governance.
Experience defining CMDB data models and configuration management frameworks.
Knowledge of enterprise architecture principles and application landscapes.
Ability to engage both technical and non-technical audiences.




Languages

Fluent English (essential)

Dutch or French (highly desirable)

Title: ML & GenAI Team Lead
Description:
Want to create a positive impact through AI?

Founded as one of the first AI companies in Belgium with a mission to create a meaningful impact, we are believers in automation and efficiency. We see Artificial Intelligence as a toolbox that will allow us to automate more of the growing list of tasks that no longer require human-level cognition.

We are a tech team specialising in AI engineering, LLM systems, and MLOps. At Sagacify, part of Craftzing, you will find a collaborative human-sized environment with a great team spirit where you can bring ideas and take responsibilities in the areas you want to evolve in, regardless of your level of experience. We strive to create innovative digital solutions with a lasting impact, in a fun atmosphere where remote work, flexible schedules and regular team events are part of our culture.

We are looking for skilled people and team players to join the adventure.

Your Role

We're on the lookout for a Mid-Level / Senior ML & GenAI Team Lead who is equally at home in pull requests as in people conversations. Someone who does not see leadership and technical depth as a trade-off, but as two things that make each other better. In this role, you will help build and steer a high-performing team of ML and AI Engineers, while staying an active contributor on client projects that actually ship. In practice your time is split roughly between 30% of leading the team and 70% of hands-on delivery.

In this role you sit at the intersection of working across teams, offices, and disciplines. You report to the Head of ML at Sagacify and collaborate closely with the Craftzing delivery organisation.

What You'll Do

Team leadership


Lead, mentor and grow a team of five ML and AI Engineers
Set and enforce quality standards, delivery frameworks and best practices
Foster a culture of continuous learning, innovation and knowledge sharing
Own key client relationships, ensuring satisfaction and long-term trust


AI Engineering


Architect end-to-end AI applications orchestrating LLMs, vector databases and APIs
Design and build autonomous agents and conversational systems
Engineer and optimise RAG pipelines connecting enterprise data to LLMs
Perform prompt and context engineering to improve output quality and reduce hallucinations
Evaluate system quality through generative AI metrics and production monitoring
Deploy and scale solutions with focus on latency, security and cost efficiency


MLOps


Design and maintain CI/CD pipelines and automated testing for model deployment
Monitor production models across drift, quality metrics and operational costs
Ensure versioning, reproducibility and safe rollout of models and AI services
Collaborate with DevOps and infrastructure teams for smooth deliveryYou stay in the code, you stay in the architecture and you also grow the people around you.


What You’ll Work With

Core technologies :


Python
OpenAI API, Hugging Face Transformers
LangChain, LangGraph, Langfuse
FastAPI or Flask, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines
Cloud GPU
AWS (S3, SQS, IAM, RDS, etc.)
Cursor


Bonus Points For Experience With


TypeScript
Azure
AI-native engineering workflows: hands-on experience using AI coding agents and AI-assisted development environments (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Copilot, etc.), including context engineering, subagent orchestra…


About You


You have 5 to 9+ years of experience, including a minimum 2-3 years in a tech lead role in ML/AI projects
You have excellent communication skills, you're able to present to C-level stakeholders and facilitating workshops comfortably
You have strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple work-streams simultaneously
You have the ability to coach and grow talent while remaining individually impactful on delivery
You're curious and have a genuine passion for AI, with the ability to evaluate and adopt new tools quickly
You can communicate fluently in Dutch, English and French, or at least the first two languages


Don’t worry if you don’t tick every single box, what matters most is the right mindset and a drive to learn. If you think we’re a match, we’d love to hear from you.

Why You Will Love Working With Us

You'll be part of a team of enthusiasts who love to learn and continuously develop skills in different areas and technologies. You'll work with both the Sagacify and Craftzing teams, purposefully driven to create solutions that make a lasting difference, in an environment tailored to your needs.

Join us and contribute to the world of tomorrow by fast-tracking our customer’s AI journey in various industries.


Snacks, fruits and unlimited coffee
Net allowances, hospitalisation and group insurance
Hybrid and flexible working culture
Car & fuel or mobility budget
Pleasant working environment
Teambuildings and yearly holiday retreat


Recruiting process


Interview with the Head of Talent & Culture and the Tech Lead
Technical test
Meeting with the team (to discover the reality of the position)
Final interview & Offer

Title: Solution Engineer
Description:
About Unframe

Unframe is an AI-first startup helping the world’s largest enterprises bring LLM-powered applications to life in days - not months. We combine the speed of a product company with the flexibility of a consultancy, helping customers move from idea to deployed AI systems faster than anyone else in the market.

Backed by Bessemer, Craft, and TLV Partners with $50M in Series A funding, we’re building a fast-growing, revenue-generating company working with Fortune 500 customers globally

About The Role

As a Solutions Engineer at Unframe, you’ll be the first technical voice a prospect hears. You’ll join early conversations, showcase real solutions built on Unframe, and help potential customers connect the dots between their needs and our platform. You’ll demo AI-powered use cases across multiple industries — from finance to cybersecurity to operations — and guide technical discovery to help prospects envision what’s possible.

This is a high-impact, outward-facing role at the intersection of Sales, Product and Marketing.

What You’ll Do


Join early-stage calls with Strategic Account Executives to lead technical discovery and demos.
Deliver compelling live demos tailored to customer pain points and use cases.
Translate customer pain points into relevant AI-powered solution examples.
Communicate the value and architecture of Unframe clearly and confidently.
Build credibility and trust with prospects while becoming a strategic partner in shaping their AI vision.
Share insights with Product, Marketing, and R&D teams based on real-world conversations.


What We Are Looking For


5+ years experience in solutions engineering, technical pre-sales, or product management.
Strong understanding of enterprise software, APIs, and modern cloud platforms.
Confident presenting technical products to both business and technical audiences.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.
Curious, collaborative, and customer-obsessed — you know how to ask the right questions and build strong relationships.


Why join us?

You’ll help some of the world’s leading companies understand and adopt AI in real, practical ways. You’ll work directly with enterprise customers, lead meaningful technical conversations, and help shape how our platform is experienced in the market. You’ll have real ownership, work closely with Product, Sales, and R&D, and play a key role in building what comes next in one of the fastest-moving spaces in tech.

Title: Growth and Strategy Manager
Description:
Location: Brussels, Belgium (Hybrid)

Client Sector: Global Tech / E-commerce & Logistics




The Opportunity:

I am currently partnering with a world-renowned leader in the digital marketplace and last-mile delivery sector. Following a period of significant global growth, they are looking for a high-caliber Strategic Program Manager to act as the right hand to the Belgian Country Director.




This is not a "ivory tower" strategy role; it is a high-visibility position designed for a "doer" who can bridge the gap between long-term vision and daily operational excellence. You will be the central pivot point for the Belgian leadership team, ensuring that ambitious growth targets are translated into successful, cross-functional execution.




Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Roadmap Development: Collaborate with senior leadership to design and iterate on the national business strategy, turning high-level goals into measurable KPIs and project milestones.
Cross-Functional Project Leadership: Take full ownership of large-scale initiatives spanning logistics, marketing, and customer experience. You will move projects from initial concept through to full-scale deployment.
Data-Driven Decision Support: Act as the analytical engine for the local C-suite, providing competitive intelligence, market trend analysis, and performance deep-dives to guide commercial choices.
Operational Transformation: Identify bottlenecks in the current business model and implement scalable solutions to enhance efficiency and profitability.
Stakeholder Harmonization: Navigate a complex matrix environment, aligning local Belgian operations with global central functions and sharing best practices across border.




Who You Are:

Professional Background: You possess 3 to 6 years of experience within Tier-1 Strategy Consulting, Corporate Development, or a high-growth "Scale-up" environment.
Analytical Rigor: You are comfortable with complex data sets and financial modeling, but you know how to distill that data into a compelling narrative for executives.
Execution Focus: You have a proven track record of managing complex projects in competitive landscapes (e.g., E-commerce, FinTech, Marketplace, or FMCG).
Communication Style: You are a persuasive communicator who can influence without direct authority. Fluency in English is essential; proficiency in French and/or Dutch will significantly set you apart.
Mindset: You thrive in fast-moving, sometimes ambiguous environments and are comfortable "getting your hands dirty" to ensure a project crosses the finish line.




What’s in it for you?

This is a chance to join a household-name tech brand that still maintains the energy of a startup. You will have a front-row seat to how a global platform scales, with a culture that prioritizes diversity, professional growth, and internal mobility. My client offers a competitive salary package, flexible working arrangements, and the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the Belgian digital landscape.




Interested?

If you are ready to drive the next chapter of growth for a global tech leader, please submit your CV for a confidential discussion.

  • Execution Focus: You have a proven track record of managing complex projects in competitive landscapes (e.g., E-commerce, FinTech, Marketplace, or FMCG).
  • Communication Style: You are a persuasive communicator who can influence without direct authority. Fluency in English is essential; proficiency in French and/or Dutch will significantly set you apart.
  • Mindset: You thrive in fast-moving, sometimes ambiguous environments and are comfortable "getting your hands dirty" to ensure a project crosses the finish line.


What’s in it for you?

This is a chance to join a household-name tech brand that still maintains the energy of a startup. You will have a front-row seat to how a global platform scales, with a culture that prioritizes diversity, professional growth, and internal mobility. My client offers a competitive salary package, flexible working arrangements, and the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the Belgian digital landscape.


Interested?

If you are ready to drive the next chapter of growth for a global tech leader, please submit your CV for a confidential discussion.

Title: Project Engineer - Acoustics & Vibrations
Description:

The Project Engineer role in the Acoustics & Vibrations department is crucial for providing expertise in managing noise and vibration issues, ensuring high acoustic quality in diverse projects ranging from buildings to infrastructure.

Assist with specific noise and vibration measurements.
Analyse and report data to obtain indicators per standards and legislation.
Model and simulate noise and vibration generation and propagation using specialized software.
Collaborate with clients to find optimal solutions.
Ensure that projects are delivered on budget, on time, and meet quality standards and H&S rules.

Nice to Have:


Dynamic interpersonal and communication skills.
Creative problem-solving capabilities.
Experience in operational noise and vibration impact mitigation strategies.


Other Details:


Location: Mainly situated in Belgium, with projects worldwide.

Work Environment: Involves various sectors including buildings, infrastructure, and nuclear facilities.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Artificialy is an AI company based in Switzerland, with offices in Lugano and Zurich. We design and deliver AI solutions that are impactful, transparent, and tailored to real-world industry needs.




Founded in 2020 by pioneers with over 40 years of combined experience in the field, our team brings together talented engineers, physicists, and mathematicians from across Europe. We are driven by curiosity, technical excellence, and a shared ambition to build AI systems that make a tangible difference.




We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer who will work with clients in the financial sector to design, develop, and operationalize data-driven solutions.




For this role you must be willing to work at least 80% onsite in our office and potentially on client’s site.




About the Role

Your responsibilities will span the full lifecycle of ML initiatives spanning from early experimentation to deployment, monitoring, and continuous optimization.
Projects may involve automated or agentic analytical pipelines, forecasting models, anomaly-detection systems, or other statistical and machine-learning solutions.
Much of the work will run within cloud-based environments, where you will ensure that pipelines and models are reliable, scalable, and aligned with the high standards typical in banking.
You will collaborate with software engineering, infrastructure, and domain teams to integrate solutions smoothly into enterprise ecosystems.




Qualifications and Skills

Master’s degree or PhD in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, Informatics, Engineering, or equivalent discipline.
2+ years of experience as ML Engineer or similar
Strong programming skills (Python, SQL, …) and familiarity with ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, etc.).
Solid understanding of ML principles, statistical modeling, and modern data-processing techniques.
Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and with deploying ML systems in scalable, production-grade environments.
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Matplotlib).
Familiarity with Git-based development workflows (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket).
Proficiency in English.




Nice to have

Familiarity with cloud ML services and hybrid cloud architectures (e.g., AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, Vertex AI).
Knowledge of deployment and orchestration tools (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD).
Understanding of data systems and architectures (e.g., PostgreSQL, distributed data frameworks).
Proficiency in Italian or German; French is a plus.




We offer

Full-time permanent contract
Competitive compensation and growth opportunities
A stimulating scientific environment with an informal working atmosphere
Mentorship and continuous learning

Title: Data Scientist - Business Intelligence
Description:
Our Brand:

MCM is a luxury lifestyle goods and fashion house founded in 1976 with an attitude defined by the cultural Zeitgeist and its German heritage with a focus on functional innovation, including the use of cutting-edge techniques. Today, through its association with music, art, travel, and technology, MCM embodies the bold, rebellious and aspirational. Always with an eye on the disruptive, the driving force behind MCM centers on revolutionizing classic design with futuristic materials. Appealing to the 21st Century Global Nomad generation - MCM’s millennial and Gen Z audience is genderless, ageless, empowered and unconstrained by rules and boundaries.




MCM is currently distributed online and in 650 stores worldwide including Munich, Berlin, Zurich, London, Paris, New York, Hong Kong, Shanghai, Beijing, Seoul, Tokyo, Middle East and more.




For further information about MCM: www.mcmworldwide.com




Working with us:

With a highly collaborative workforce working from offices and stores around the world, MCM established a multinational/multicultural organization. To uphold the culture & value of MCM, we intend to fulfil our corporate social responsibility by implementing the following values and to comply with high degree of legitimacy and ethical standards.




Our employees make the best efforts to become high-performing individuals who reflect the diversity of the communities in which we work and live. MCM’s commitment to diversity and inclusion includes recruiting and retaining employees from diverse backgrounds and experiences, creating awareness of diversity issues and benefits, and fostering a supportive environment where inclusivity is expected and prioritized. We have zero tolerance in any form of harassment, insultation, ostracization or groundless defamation of any other person.




Accountabilities

We are looking for a Data Scientist – Business Intelligence to join our Business Technology & Intelligence team. This role sits at the heart of our evolving data ecosystem. You will play a key role in building and scaling our data foundation within Databricks, integrating multiple enterprise data sources, and enabling advanced analytics and machine learning capabilities across the business.

You will work in a fast-paced, agile environment where ownership, initiative, and collaboration are essential. This is a hands-on role where you will take business requests from concept through to production—building pipelines, developing models, and enabling actionable insights across the organisation.




Key Responsibilities




Design, build, and maintain end-to-end data pipelines in Databricks, integrating data from multiple systems including Saleforce products (SFMC, and SFCC) , SAP, and the clienteling platform
Support and deliver ad-hoc deep-dive analysis
Develop, deploy, and maintain production-grade machine learning models, including: customer segmentation, churn and retention prediction, customer lifetime value (CLV) modelling, sales forecasting, marketing attribution and campaign effectiveness, Lead scoring and CRM modelling and personalisation and recommendation use cases.
Ensure production reliability, performance, and scalability of data pipelines and ML solutions
Improve and expand our Customer 360 data model, identifying gaps in data availability, quality, and integration.
Continuously improve data quality, accessibility, and usability across the organisation
Support data aggregation, standardisation, and transformation logic for CRM and marketing use cases
Translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions in collaboration with stakeholders across global digital, CRM/marketing, merchandising, and commercial teams
Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and KPI reporting frameworks to support business decision-making




Requirements

Bachelor’s degree or higher in Statistics, Machine Learning, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
2–4 years of experience in data science, data analytics, or a related field
Thrives in a fast-moving environment and enjoys building from the ground up
Comfortable working across technical and business domains, turning complex requirements into scalable data and ML solutions
Strong communicator, confident in a multicultural, cross-functional organisation
Proven production experience in Databricks, including building and maintaining data pipelines
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python
Experience building and deploying machine learning models in production environments
Strong understanding of data modelling, data integration, and ETL/ELT pipelines
Experience with Power BI or similar BI tools
Comfortable working in fast-paced, agile environments with evolving requirements
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Fluent in English (mandatory)
Experience working with customer data: Salesforce CRM (SFDC), Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC)
Exposure to SAP or enterprise retail/eCommerce data ecosystems
Experience in retail, luxury, or consumer-facing industries
Knowledge of marketing analytics, CRM automation, or clienteling tools

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
About the role

Imagine building intelligent systems that don't just work in perfect lab conditions, but perform reliably in the real world, where uncertainty is the norm and failure is not an option. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will push the limits of computer vision by designing, training, and validating neural networks that operate in complex, safety-critical environments. Your work will directly influence how autonomous systems perceive and interact with the world, combining deep learning, simulation, and rigorous verification into one seamless pipeline.

At Destinus, we are shaping the future of defense through high-speed, autonomous flight technologies. We develop next-generation unmanned aerial systems that deliver greater speed, precision, and efficiency, supporting governments and defense organizations in mission-critical operations worldwide. Daedalean AG, the AI and autonomy hub of the Destinus Group, plays a key role in this vision. Developing safety-critical AI systems at the intersection of advanced research and real-world aviation. Enabling machines to perceive, understand, and make decisions in real time. Together, we combine aerospace engineering and artificial intelligence to build the next generation of autonomous flight systems, driving innovation where it matters most.


What You´ll Do

Design, train, and optimize deep learning models for computer vision tasks in highly dynamic and safety-critical environments
Own the full ML lifecycle, from data strategy and model architecture to deployment and performance monitoring
Develop robust evaluation pipelines to ensure models meet strict reliability and safety requirements
Work on ML certification activities, validating model behavior under defined regulatory frameworks
Leverage transfer learning and simulation to scale training and testing beyond real-world data limitations
Collaborate closely with software, systems, and flight teams to integrate ML models into real-world applications
Continuously push model performance across edge cases, environmental variability, and operational constraints



Requirements



What You'll Need

Strong programming skills in C++ or Rust
Master's or PhD in computer science, physics, mathematics, or a related technical field
At least 5 years of hands-on experience in deep learning for computer vision
Experience across the full ML stack, including model design, training pipelines, and evaluation systems
Solid understanding of data-centric AI approaches, including dataset curation and augmentation strategies
Experience with simulation environments or synthetic data generation is a strong plus
Proven ability to tackle complex research problems over extended periods, in both academic and industrial settings


Who You Are

You thrive in environments where problems are hard, ambiguous, and worth solving. You are not just training models, you are engineering systems that must work every time. You think long-term, validate everything, and take ownership of performance in the real world. When others stop at good enough, you keep pushing until it is reliable, scalable, and certifiable.

Title: Junior Data Scientist 100% (f/m/d)
Description:
At Julius Baer, we celebrate and value the individual qualities you bring, enabling you to be impactful, to be entrepreneurial, to be empowered, and to create value beyond wealth. Let’s shape the future of wealth management together.

Do you aspire to be part of a high performing Analytics and Transformation team within a global Compliance organisation at the forefront of the wealth management industry? In this diverse role you will implement new technologies, manage change initiatives, develop predictive models, and perform financial and economic analysis to increase the efficiency and effectiveness of risk management. Working alongside Risk, Compliance, Analytics and Legal SMEs in highly skilled, fast-paced teams you will drive business value through collaboratively solving complex analytical problems.

YOUR CHALLENGE


Lead and support data analytics and business intelligence initiatives to success, with focus on Non-Financial Risk (e.g., Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Client Review or Sanctions)
Collaborate with internal IT and external providers to conduct in-depth analyses and identify best-in-class solutions by leveraging latest technology as well as the Compliance Analytics & Transformation in-house built infrastructure
Design, develop, monitor and document predictive models following industry best practices and sound theoretical foundation
Work closely with senior stakeholders to design quantitative solutions using different analytical principles to optimise various banking processes
Assess and manage change requests raised by the business
Execute regular and ad-hoc analysis and reports


YOUR PROFILE


Master or Bachelor’s degree from a university of applied sciences in STEM fields (science, technology, engineering, mathematics)
2-3 years of Banking experience with focus on Compliance, Risk Management and Legal
Strong communication skills with the ability to decompose complex solutions and data insights into a compelling storyline for the rest of the organisation
As an agile worker, you enjoy working in a fast-paced environment where things evolve quickly
Enthusiastic about leveraging data and statistics to solve complex problems
Curious to learn new things and apply out-of-the-box thinking to challenge the status quo and proactively propose new solutions
Team-oriented and enjoy collaborating with diverse experts, listening to different ideas and voicing your own, to deliver the best possible outcome
Strong database programming skills (e.g. SQL)
Proficient in programming (e.g. in Python, R, Java, or C++)
Experience in project management or business analysis
Fluent in English (main business language), any further language is an advantage


We are looking forward to receiving your full job application through our online application tool. Further interesting job opportunities can be found on our Career site.

Title: Data Scientist (W/M)
Description:
About The Swiss Data Science Center (SDSC)

The Swiss Data Science Center (SDSC) is a national research infrastructure of the ETH domain in data science and artificial intelligence (AI), with EPFL and ETH Zurich as founding partners. Its mandate is to support academic labs, hospitals, industry and public sector stakeholders, including cantonal and federal administrations, through their entire data science journey, from the collection and management of data to machine learning, AI, and industrialization. With a large multidisciplinary team of professionals across three locations (Lausanne, Zurich, Villigen), the SDSC provides expertise and services across domains such as health and biomedical sciences, energy and sustainability, climate and environment, and large-scale scientific infrastructure.

The candidate will be integrated into a dynamic and rich environment, with people from different fields and expertise, and will be part of SDSC's Research team. The Research team at SDSC comprises more than 35 data scientists, seeking to apply novel AI/ML methods to solve real-world problems in the academic and public sectors. See https://datascience.ch/projects for an idea of some of our academic research collaborations.

Mission

As a Data Scientist working in the Research team of the Swiss Data Science Center, you will help researchers and other collaborators in academia or the public sector in Switzerland to leverage state-of-the-art Machine Learning and AI methods. These collaborators encompass various fields and carry out projects involving diverse data (tabular, textual, visual, potentially multi-modal), notably in health and biomedical sciences, climate and environment, energy and sustainability, and social sciences.

This typically involves exchanging actively with collaborators and domain experts to understand the precise desiderata of the project, determining which approaches, formulations, and models are most effective to achieve the desired goals, implementing the corresponding algorithms, performing the evaluation hand-in-hand with collaborators, and releasing open-source code and writing research papers when appropriate.

Main Duties And Responsibilities Include


Working on data science projects with collaborators from the academic and public sector
Engaging with diverse stakeholders, in particular researchers across various domains and other professionals
Preparing scientific publications for top-tier domain conferences and journals in collaboration with Senior Data Scientists
Potentially supervising students at different levels in collaboration with Senior Data Scientists


Your profile


MSc in a Data Science-related field, or in Computer Science with a strong background in Machine Learning and AI
Experience in designing/selecting and training Machine Learning models for different types of data, including tabular, textual, visual, and potentially multi-modal
Proficiency in Python and PyTorch
Experience in applying data science and AI in interdisciplinary research or industrial collaborations
Creative mindset and good concrete problem solving skills


We offer

Work Environment


A stimulating, dynamic, diverse, and cross-disciplinary environment
Nice offices located on the EPFL and on the Biopole campuses


Professional development


Opportunities to broaden your expertise in ML and AI targeted to diverse application domains
Collaborations with experts in both Data Science and domain sciences
Opportunities to publish contributions to research projects in high-impact venues
Possibility to travel and present work in international venues


Informations

Contract Start Date : 01/08/2026

Activity Rate : 100.00

Contract Type: CDD

Duration: 1 year renewable

Reference: 2270

Contact

Please note that we exclusively accept applications submitted through our online application portal. Applications via email or postal services will not be considered.

Questions regarding the position should be directed to Mathieu Salzmann (mathieu.salzmann@epfl.ch) or Guillaume Obozinski (guillaume.obozinski@sdsc.ethz.ch) (no applications).

We would like to point out that the pre-selection is carried out by the responsible recruiters and not by artificial intelligence.

  • Potentially supervising students at different levels in collaboration with Senior Data Scientists

Your profile

  • MSc in a Data Science-related field, or in Computer Science with a strong background in Machine Learning and AI
  • Experience in designing/selecting and training Machine Learning models for different types of data, including tabular, textual, visual, and potentially multi-modal
  • Proficiency in Python and PyTorch
  • Experience in applying data science and AI in interdisciplinary research or industrial collaborations
  • Creative mindset and good concrete problem solving skills

We offer

Work Environment

  • A stimulating, dynamic, diverse, and cross-disciplinary environment
  • Nice offices located on the EPFL and on the Biopole campuses

Professional development

  • Opportunities to broaden your expertise in ML and AI targeted to diverse application domains
  • Collaborations with experts in both Data Science and domain sciences
  • Opportunities to publish contributions to research projects in high-impact venues
  • Possibility to travel and present work in international venues

Informations

Contract Start Date : 01/08/2026

Activity Rate : 100.00

Contract Type: CDD

Duration: 1 year renewable

Reference: 2270

Contact

Please note that we exclusively accept applications submitted through our online application portal. Applications via email or postal services will not be considered.

Questions regarding the position should be directed to Mathieu Salzmann (mathieu.salzmann@epfl.ch) or Guillaume Obozinski (guillaume.obozinski@sdsc.ethz.ch) (no applications).

We would like to point out that the pre-selection is carried out by the responsible recruiters and not by artificial intelligence.

Title: Data Scientist – HR Transformation & AI Automation
Description:
Established in 2004, Mercuria is one of the leading integrated energy and commodity trading companies in the world. We bring energy markets together to support the needs of today by trading, structuring finance, and investing into strategic assets, while generating more than $100 billion in turnover.







Our operations span over 50 countries on 5 continents, including all the major energy hubs. We trade physical oil, energy products, environmental products and other commodities from Geneva, London, Singapore, Shanghai, Beijing, Dubai, Houston, Calgary and Greenwich (CT).







We are committed to advancing the transition to a more sustainable, affordable and reliable energy system for tomorrow. Over 50% of our assets are in low carbon & energy transition sectors - providing a strong platform to trade these new markets and support decarbonization.




In 2023, we established Silvania, a $500 million fund, investing into restoration and protection of nature and biodiversity globally and in support of the Paris Agreement goals & UN 30x30 biodiversity initiative. This new asset class supports nature protection and provides sustainable financial returns.




The Role




We are looking for a Data Scientist to join the HR team and drive the transformation of HR processes through data, automation, and Artificial Intelli

gence.This role will focus on identifying inefficiencies, improving workflows, and implementing AI-driven solutions to automate repetitive HR tasks and enhance decision-making across the employee lifecycle. The successful candidate will act as a bridge between HR and technology, helping build a more data-driven, scalable, and efficient HR function.




Key Responsibilities




Analyze existing HR processes and identify opportunities for automation and opti
mization.Design, develop, and implement AI-powered tools and workflows to improve HR op
erations.Automate repetitive administrative tasks across recruitment, onboarding, reporting, employee data management, and other HR ac
tivities.Develop dashboards, predictive models, and data analytics solutions to support HR decisio
n-making.Collaborate closely with HR stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into technical s
olutions.Work with internal IT and data teams to ensure proper integration, governance, and security of
HR data.Monitor and improve the performance of implemented automation and AI s
olutions. Stay up to date with emerging AI technologies and assess their potential applications within HR.




Profile & Qualifications




Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
Proven experience in data science, machine learning, process automation, or AI implementation.
Strong programming skills in Python, SQL, and data visualization tools.
Experience with AI/LLM tools, workflow automation platforms, and predictive analytics.
Ability to translate business needs into practical and scalable technical solutions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
Previous exposure to HR systems, HR analytics, or people data is a strong advantage.




What We Are Looking For




A proactive and innovative mindset.
Strong interest in AI-driven transformation.
Ability to work autonomously while collaborating with cross-functional teams.
Someone who can challenge existing processes and bring modern solutions to improve efficiency and user experience within HR.
Fluent English

Title: Data Science Specialist
Description:
At Nestlé Nutrition, our mission is to nurture a Healthier Generation. We aim to achieve this by leading the way in Research and Development of science proven nutrition solutions and by partnering with parents and healthcare professionals around the world. From pre-conception to adulthood, we are widening our reach and opening up new opportunities across the lifelong nutrition journey.

Position Snapshot

Location: Konolfingen, Switzerland

Company: Nestlé Product & Technology Center (NPTC) Nutrition

Act. Rate: Full-Time Act. Rate 100%

Type of contract: Permanent contract

What We Offer At Nestlé

Exciting opportunities to develop your career your way; across different brands, businesses, functions, and regions.

Flexible working arrangements - facilitating creativity and collaboration

A culture of respect, with diversity, equity and inclusion at its core. Learn more here.

A dynamic international environment empowering you to learn, develop and grow.

Don’t hesitate to connect with us during the recruitment process to learn more.

Position Summary

As a Data Science Specialist, you will lead predictive modeling initiatives and translate complex data into actionable insights to support product innovation within the Nutrition Innovation Unit. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams (Scientists, Sensory Specialist, Product Developers, and others) to ensure robust data connectivity, high analytical standards, and full compliance with company frameworks.

A Day in the Life of a Data Science Specialist


Lead the application of machine learning techniques (e.g., regression, classification, time-series forecasting) to solve complex business problems across domains such as product development and advanced analytics
Own the preparation of complex datasets, including data cleaning, transformation, and structuring, to enable robust modeling and high‑quality analysis.
Partner closely with domain experts and cross‑functional stakeholders to design, develop, and deliver impactful data science solutions aligned with business needs.
Deploy and operationalize models in production environments, ensuring performance, reliability, and scalability, using platforms such as Azure ML, Databricks, and Snowflake.
Monitor model performance and continuously optimize accuracy, robustness, and scalability over time.
Translate analytical insights into clear visualizations and structured narratives, effectively communicating recommendations to both technical and non‑technical audiences.


What Will Make You Successful


Advanced degree (MSc or higher) in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field
Extensive experience in data science within Life Sciences, Food, or Academia, applying agile development practices
Proven track record in building and deploying predictive models in production
Hands-on expertise with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, Scikit-learn) and statistical methods including Design of Experiments
Familiarity with APIs (REST, JSON) and data visualization tools such as Power BI, PowerApps, and Streamlit
Proficiency with cloud and data platforms including Azure, Databricks, Snowflake, and SQL databases.
Ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical audience
Strong collaboration skills, with experience working closely with cross-functional teams (scientists, sensory specialists, product developers)
Highly autonomous and self‑driven, with strong problem‑solving capabilities
Excellent communication skills in English


Nestlé is committed to the accessibility of its recruitment process. If you require an adaptation during the recruitment process, please do let us know so we can support you effectively. All information will be treated confidentially.

At Nestlé Research and Development in Konolfingen near Bern / Switzerland, we develop for Nestlé worldwide, new products, processes and packaging concepts for a broad range of products of our Nutrition, Dairy, Health Science and Coffee businesses. In addition, we also provide technical assistance to our factories.

#nestlecareerswitzerland
